# Plan-review verification gate (open→in_progress)
## Context
rebar has a completion-verifier *close* gate (verifies a ticket's work was actually done before close). It has
no equivalent at the *start* of work: when an agent claims a ticket (open→in_progress), nothing checks that the
ticket's implementation plan is sound before the agent executes it. Flawed plans (too big to one-shot, ambiguous,
wrong file targets, ignored consumers, missing tests, criteria that don't match the work, children that don't
cover the parent, unvalidated novel architecture) get executed anyway, and early errors compound over autonomous
agent trajectories (~44% of agent failures are spec/plan defects).
This epic builds a **plan-review verification gate** that fires at open→in_progress — the inverse of the
completion-verifier. It is a rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE (many languages, frameworks,
project structures, and ticket-creation styles), so it must be polyglot, fail-open on unsupported stacks, and
flexible about inconsistently-scoped tickets. Its posture is to **COACH** agents to author better plans via
positive, well-formed guidance — not to nitpick or roadblock — while emitting a signed **certification** that a
review process was followed (a composable "rigorous agentic development vs vibe-coding" signal for CI).
Full design detail lives in session log `63cc-ec94-4792-464b` (plan-of-record v4 + the ~30 research reports +
the discussion narrative). This description is the self-contained epic summary.
## Prerequisite foundations — NOW SHIPPED (2026-06)
Three prerequisites this epic was written to build ON have since landed. The design below is
UNCHANGED, but its supporting machinery is now CONCRETE, not aspirational — where the text says
'build/add X', read 'consume the shipped X'. (We are not reopening the closed prerequisite epics;
this section + the per-child notes realign THIS epic to what they delivered.)
- **Code-grounding evidence oracle — epic `8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82` (CLOSED).** `src/rebar/grounding/`
ships a polyglot, fail-open, THREE-VALUED evidence oracle behind the `refute_absence` / `applies` /
`scan` facade (exported verbatim from `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py`): T0 dependency existence (deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), T1 symbol/import/
file resolution (universal-ctags, 164 langs, no server), Engine B detectors (OpenGrep/ast-grep +
scc/lizard metrics), plus the static `grounding_info` contract (closed dimension-id / reference-kind
/ abstain-reason vocab + detected backend versions). Layer-1 (P2/P3/P4) CONSUMES this oracle — it no
longer adds solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations. The T2 LSP-semantic-resolution
follow-on is epic `850f-bf87-8600-4af6` (open), not required for v1.
- **LLM workflow engine — epic `e6d9-41da-da0d-44ef` (CLOSED, 'Workflow engine v2').**
`src/rebar/llm/workflow/` ships the declarative IR + interpreter/executor, `run_workflow` (CLI +
write-gated MCP tool, mirroring `verify_completion`), the PydanticAI runner + the structured-output
reliability stack, and the Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`). The gate runs AS a
workflow on this engine; the standing-eval-suite AC wires into the shipped eval seam.
- **Prompt storage / contract-bearing prompt model — epic `da27-c916-f04e-4885` (all children CLOSED;
epic closed).** `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` ships the front-matter CLOSED-key contract model
(`inputs`/`outputs` as schema-registry NAMES, `execution_mode` {single_turn|agentic}, `category`),
the derived prompt index + CI drift gate, `.rebar/prompts/<id>.md` override resolution (project >
built-in), and the canonical front-matter writer + in-editor authoring/write-back. The criteria
registry's 'reuse rebar.llm catalog/contracts/prompt/runner' is now exactly this machinery: each
criterion ships as a contract-bearing prompt declaring its exec tier; production prompts live in the
prompt library, not the experiment `criteria_v8.json`.
Per-child realignment: Layer-1 `012e` consumes the oracle facade; orchestrator `ca03` builds on the
shipped engine + prompt library; eval-suite `7284` wires the shipped Inspect-AI eval seam.
NOTE on `criteria_v8.json`: it remains the canonical DESIGN descriptor set + the input to the prototype
harness (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/final_gate_3pass.py`); PRODUCTION criteria graduate
into the shipped prompt library as contract-bearing prompts.
## Design summary
- **Trigger / enforcement:** fires at open→in_progress. `claim` is a FAST signature-verify check only (no LLM, no
network round-trip; target p95 < ~50ms). The heavy review runs OUT-OF-BAND via a dedicated entrypoint
(`rebar review-plan <ticket>` / an MCP `run_workflow` tool, mirroring `rebar verify-completion`), triggered by
the agent on a claim-block or by CI. A REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the client revises and re-runs to earn a
fresh signature (mirrors completion-verifier). Routing pivots on **has_children + complexity + ticket LEVEL/TYPE** (proportionate scrutiny — see below), NOT ticket type alone.
- **Two layers:** LAYER 1 = a minimal, deterministic, structural floor that BLOCKS (frozen, unambiguous, cheap,
polyglot-where-tooling-exists, fail-open; convergent by construction). LAYER 2 = an ADVISORY coaching review
(per-criterion judgment + one bounded broad open-ended pass) that SURFACES findings authored for remediation
and never blocks by default.
- **Verdict model — THREE-PASS (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate; see the dedicated section
below):** the model does NOT emit severity or confidence. Pass 1 surfaces grounded FINDINGS; a SEPARATE Pass-2
verifier emits severity attributes + atomic binary sub-answers; a DETERMINISTIC Pass 3 computes confidence +
severity and decides `block | advisory | dropped`. A review-level INDETERMINATE remains for budget/tool-unavailable.
Defaults ship advisory in v1, but blocking is NOT DET-only: the DET floor blocks structurally AND each
LLM-tier criterion can block when a project opts it in / sets its `block_threshold` (we ship thresholds
HIGH so almost everything is advisory during calibration). Per-criterion blocking + a `--force`-override
are project-configurable. This REPLACES the earlier per-criterion PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL+model-severity model (adopted from
the shared three-pass framework, epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b`).
- **Coaching / feedback spec:** the reviewer SUPPLIES the finding + location the agent can't self-detect
(research: LLMs can't FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once located). Each advisory finding is grounded
in a criterion/file/section, specific + actionable, severity-ranked + capped, affirms what already passes, a
suggested fix only when confident, expressed as a checklist the agent addresses.
- **Pass-4 affirmative COACH (advisory; single-turn, no tooling):** a fourth pass AFTER Pass-3, over only the
SURVIVING (advisory, not-dropped) findings, priming the caller with a productive NEXT MOVE per finding — beyond
returning findings (the gate's T2 can flag 'should spike' as a *finding*, but coaching turns surviving findings
into forward MOVES). It is a SINGLE-TURN STRUCTURED-OUTPUT call (NOT an agent): it maps each finding to a move
from a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY and extracts a short subject — output `{move_id, subject,
finding_refs[]}`, clustered by move — and the coaching prose is rendered DETERMINISTICALLY from the move's
locked template (the LLM never authors free prose, so it cannot reflect-prompt, hand a solution, restate
findings, or flatter). LLM-recipient guardrails (positive-by-construction): C1 each note is actionable GUIDANCE
— a move to pursue (run a spike, research prior art, weigh an alternative), NOT executable code/commands or the
solution (enforced by a SUBJECT VALIDATOR — `subject` is a bounded noun-phrase, code/imperatives rejected — so
C1 holds mechanically, not merely via the template); C2 reference findings BY ID (caller-visible finding `id`, no restatement); C3 select-from-registry +
fill-subject (the template enforces name-the-move-not-the-answer); C4 cap/cluster/front-load. Moves: 1 spike ·
2 prior-art/OSS-research · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives · 6 specification-
by-example · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door · 9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-
children · 12 generalize-the-finding-across-the-work. Grounded in LLM-feedback research (external-grounded >
self-reflection; anti-sycophancy; lost-in-the-middle). Coaching is ADVISORY (never blocks) and applies equally
to the `9da1` code reviewers — see the Pass-4 coach child.
- **Unified priority score + advisory surfacing CAP (config-driven; default 10):** validity and impact are BOTH
LLM-judged spectrums (neither absolute), so Pass-3 combines them DETERMINISTICALLY into ONE priority score (not
two separate gates) — used for both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (a low-validity/high-impact
finding can outrank a high-validity/low-impact one). The surfaced ADVISORY (non-blocking) findings are capped at
the top-N by that score, N = a CONFIG value (default **10**, owned by `55de`); advisory overflow beyond N -> the
`REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar, not surfaced (volume is the lever that preserves an LLM's action — the cap, not the
label). **The cap NEVER drops BLOCKING findings — blocking is EXEMPT; if blocking findings exceed N, ALL of them
are returned** (the cap can never weaken the block decision). Default 10 is grounded by a cap-dilution experiment
(high-value remediation held ~0.99->0.91 across surfaced lists of 4-32 for a strong single-pass reviser -> 10 is
well inside the no-degradation zone). EXPERIMENT LIMITATIONS — valuable but INCONCLUSIVE ALONE: it did NOT test a
smaller model (e.g. Sonnet) and did NOT run under CONTEXT PRESSURE (a common trigger for agents shortcutting /

ignoring feedback); 10 is a config DEFAULT to TUNE from real-world dogfood data, not a validated constant. Each surfaced finding carries a stable `id` — a content fingerprint MINTED by the orchestrator (`ca03`) when it
assembles the post-Pass-3 result, its SOLE owner; the Pass-4 coach (`75a9`) and the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar
(`db7b`) reference findings BY this id (never restating them).
- **Iteration-fatigue failure mode (why the cap + never-drop-blocking matter):** LLMs get frustrated across review
rounds — if the 3rd/4th round keeps surfacing NEW findings, the model tends to try to FORCE PAST a blocked gate
rather than keep addressing feedback. A bounded, prioritized, convergent advisory surface mitigates this; and
because blocking findings are never dropped by the cap, the block decision is never weakened to placate a
fatigued reviser.
- **Criteria registry:** each criterion is a declarative descriptor carrying `exec` (DET | 1-TURN | 2-STEP |
AGENT) + `facet` (the concern/context it examines) + `context_needs` + `routing` + `trigger`; a generic
chunker reads these (reuse rebar.llm's catalog/contracts/prompt/runner) to bucket by tier and pack same-facet
criteria into chunks — no hand-maintained chunk lists. Frozen within a review loop (convergence), grown between versions.

- **Polyglot tooling (fail-open IRONCLAD) — CONSUMES the shipped code-grounding oracle (prereq `8f6c`, CLOSED):** Layer-1 does NOT add its own tool integrations; it calls the code-grounding evidence oracle (`src/rebar/grounding/`, the `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` facade) delivered by the now-closed prerequisite epic `8f6c`. The oracle supplies, polyglot + fail-open: symbol/import/file resolution (T1, universal-ctags), package existence (T0, deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM), and size/smell scans (Engine B — OpenGrep/ast-grep + scc/lizard). Its three-valued, abstain-by-default contract IS the fail-open guarantee (unsupported lang / no tool / parse-error / timeout → `abstain` with a recorded reason → skipped = PASS, never FAIL; the recorded abstain set IS the coverage). LSP/`solidlsp` semantic resolution is the T2 follow-on (epic `850f`, open), NOT v1.
- **Execution (exec-tier-aware):** DET criteria run as parallel code (~free). SINGLE-TURN criteria are the only
chunked tier — packed by `facet` at `base_chunk(model) × size_factor(ticket)` (config-overridable; default Opus
~all-in-one cached call, Sonnet 6 coherent / 3 plain, halved for large/epic/has_children). AGENT (tool-using)
criteria run ONE agent each (≈85× a single-turn call) and are gated by proportionate scrutiny. Prompt caching on
(system+plan prefix) makes fine chunking cheap. Depth scales with complexity + has_children; escalation by
size + complexity + centrality (computed at plan time). Per-plan budget cap, chunk-atomic checkpointing;
INDETERMINATE on cap-hit sheds AGENT/overlay criteria first. Defaults grounded by experiment (see below).

- **Proportionate scrutiny (by LEVEL + TYPE; experiment-grounded round 4):** criteria carry an `applies_at` field; the orchestrator filters before chunking so leaf-implementation criteria (E6 proving-commands, G1G2/E4/A1, leaf overlays T5a/b/c) do NOT run at epic/story altitude, container criteria (G3/G4 child coverage) run only when has_children, and TYPE rules apply (bugs EXEMPT→follow-on; E5 suppressed on RED/GREEN test tasks; F4 N/A on internal/mechanical leaf tasks). Without this the gate over-fires on mid-level stories and mechanical tasks.

- **Overlay triggering (deterministic-where-low-FP, else LLM-router; validated round 4):** T1 prior-art, T5a performance, and T5d accessibility fire from low-FP deterministic signals (bright-lines / UI-keyword count); T8 LLM-antipatterns, T6 UX, T5b reliability, T5c security, T9 shared-state are LLM-routed (a keyword trigger is high-FP for them — e.g. T8 keyword-matches almost every LLM ticket). T8 is in the active set for LLM/agent-design plans (it recovers structural-gap signal a generic checklist misses). FINALIZE CORRECTION (E4-confirmed): the new resource overlays T10/T11/T12 are LLM-ROUTED for PLAN review, NOT deterministic — on a held-out non-DSO corpus the deterministic keyword over-fired (rebar 'migration' = the bash→Python strangler, not a data-migration: det T11 fired 2/19 plans, all false; T12 'deploy'/'rollback' fired 7/19, all false; the LLM router fired 0). On a code-review DIFF the file-glob triggers (the 9da1 overlay catalog) are high-precision; on a PLAN there is no diff, so route by LLM.
- **Attestation:** signed result = "process followed to the best of our ability, no red flags + coverage" — NOT
"perfect". Bound to the latest MATERIAL event commit (description/AC/file_impact/decomposition edits invalidate;
tags/comments/links/assignee do not). The cert is the composable CI rigor signal.
- **Observability / FP substrate (cheap, in-scope):** a reducer-IGNORED SIDECAR `REVIEW_RESULT` event on the
ticket (out of hot paths) capturing per-criterion verdicts + finding fingerprints + metadata + override
justifications; the signature stays a normal head-state event. Offline replay+join (ticket_id + fingerprints +
existing EDIT/STATUS/completion-verification events) yields per-criterion fire/remediation/FP/FN rates. Capture
only; analysis is offline; no human-feedback requirement.
- **Bugs are SKIPPED** in this epic (different shape; dedicated follow-on).
## Review structure — three-pass (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate)
Adopted from epic `9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b` (the shared three-pass framework for all rebar reviews) + the
code-review plan-of-record (session log `9dba-8e84-0d0e-4af8`). It **replaces per-criterion model-emitted
severity/confidence**: LLMs are unreliable at holistic severity/confidence, so neither is emitted by the model
anywhere in the decision path. The criteria registry below is the LOCKED RUBRIC the Pass-1 findings reference;
each descriptor's prose `SEVERITY:` text is now a PRIOR for the Pass-2 attribute assessment, not a model output.
- **Pass 1 — find (evidence records):** the reviewer surfaces FINDINGS as
`{finding, criteria[] (rubric ids), evidence[] (flexible: plan quote / section / ABSENCE rationale / code
citation), scenarios[], impact}` — NO severity, NO confidence. Run per facet-chunk of the rubric (the chunking
defaults still apply); PASS = no finding for a criterion; an empty list for a clean chunk is expected.
- **Pass 2 — verify (independent):** a SEPARATE verifier agent, fresh context, re-grounds in the plan (and the
code AGENTICALLY for codebase-grounded findings) and emits per finding (a) severity ATTRIBUTES
`{prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility}` (coarse ordinals) and (b) typed BINARY
sub-answers `{verdict: yes|no|insufficient}`. Rules: atomicity (one proposition/question); INDEPENDENCE (the
finding is presented as a CLAIM TO TEST, its conclusion not asserted); verdict-with-citation NOT verdict-with-fix;
`insufficient` allowed.
- **Pass 3 — decide (deterministic):** the ONLY veto is cited-code-reference-accuracy, applied ONLY when a finding
cites a specific code reference (a no-op for the non-citable absence findings that dominate plan review).
confidence (= VALIDITY) = graded fraction of the binary sub-answers (yes=1, insufficient=.5, no=0) ∈ [0,1];
IMPACT ∈ [0,1] = the deterministic mean of the Pass-2 severity attributes, each ordinal mapped to [0,1]
(prod_impact/debt_impact none/low/medium/high → 0/.33/.67/1, take the max of the two; blast_radius local/module/
system → .33/.67/1; likelihood low/med/high → .33/.67/1; reversibility easy/moderate/hard → .33/.67/1); the
severity LABEL is the same attributes bucketed (display only). UNIFIED PRIORITY SCORE = validity × impact ∈ [0,1]
— the ONE number driving both the block threshold and the advisory ranking (this attribute→[0,1] map + the
product are the v1 DEFAULT shape; weights are calibration-tunable). decision = `block | advisory | dropped`: DROP
if validity < .5; else BLOCK iff (not cited-reference-vetoed) AND priority ≥ the criterion's `block_threshold`

(default 0.95 ⇒ near-certain AND high-impact, so v1 is almost all advisory); else ADVISORY.  an LLM-tier criterion DOES block when a project opts it in / lowers its threshold; blocking is not DET-only. No model holistic severity/confidence in the path; no interactive
escalation (autonomous).
- **Pass 4 — coach (advisory; AFTER the gate, NOT in the decision path):** a single-turn structured-output
call over the SURVIVING (advisory) findings that maps each to a move from the registry and renders coaching
from a locked template — priming the caller's next revision; it NEVER alters the Pass-3 decision (see the
Design summary + child `75a9`).

- **Pass-2 vocabulary (validated against `criteria_v8`).** Severity ATTRIBUTES: `prod_impact` / `debt_impact` ∈ {none, low, medium, high}; `blast_radius` ∈ {local, module, system}; `likelihood` ∈ {low, medium, high}; `reversibility` ∈ {easy, moderate, hard}. BINARY sub-answers ∈ {yes, no, insufficient}: the VETO `cited_reference_accurate` (answered only when a code citation is present, else `na`) + the graded set {`is_verifiable`, `evidence_entails_finding`, `path_reachable`, `impact_follows_necessarily`, `no_viable_alternative_explanation`, `no_existing_mitigation`, `severity_claim_justified`}. Pass-3 maps the attributes → severity and the graded fraction → confidence.
- **Binary sub-question set (typed):** veto = cited-reference-accuracy; graded = is-verifiable,
evidence-entails-finding, path-reachable, impact-follows-necessarily, no-viable-alternative-explanation,
no-existing-mitigation, severity-claim-justified. (Research: Chain-of-Verification, FactScore/SAFE,
RAGAS-faithfulness, TICK — binary decomposition + deterministic aggregation beats a single LLM confidence number.)
- **Plan-review specialization:** evidence is flexible precisely because plan findings are often NON-citable
(an absence finding like “the plan never  … [truncated by reconciler]

content.text:
SCOPE ADD (from f81a docs review): also promote ticket_clarity.threshold to the typed Config. It is currently read ONLY from the legacy flat .rebar/config.conf via _engine_support/gates.py::_clarity_threshold -> _read_config_key (default 5, min 1), bypassing the typed Config entirely — so it is NOT settable via [tool.rebar]/rebar.toml/REBAR_<KEY> env and does not appear in `rebar config`. Add a typed key (e.g. clarity.threshold or ticket_clarity.threshold) routed through load_config, with the legacy flat key honored as a back-compat alias.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

PROGRESS — slice 1/4 done (config-loop, commit 2f8faa9d). ticket_clarity.threshold now routes through the typed Config: new typed ticket_clarity section (threshold:int=5, min 1) + read_config_file() helper for the explicit --config-file case; gates._clarity_threshold reads load_config(root)/read_config_file(file) instead of the bespoke _read_config_key (removed); legacy flat .rebar/config.conf ticket_clarity.threshold reads with NO alias (section name matches). This closes the last live .rebar/config.conf read that bypassed load_config — satisfying the epic "route ALL config-file reads through Config" AC. 10 tests; full suite green (1995); subagent review clean. REMAINING (this ticket, next iterations): (2) reconciler.* — route acli_subprocess/_advisory_lock/outbound_differ/rebar_id_audit to load_config().reconciler.* as the config-FILE layer (resolve the nice-env-name vs section-key mismatch: REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT etc. become non-deprecated canonical aliases; preserve the id-guard value-flip + fail-closed + the legacy flat rebar_id_guard_mode); (3) jira.* — JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT consumers fall back to load_config().jira.*; (4) llm.* — config-file support in LLMConfig.from_env (a [tool.rebar.llm] table, env overriding file).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

HANDOFF CHECKPOINT (config-loop, 2026-06-17). STATE: main @ 2f8faa9d, store clean (fsck OK), full offline suite green (1995 passed), ruff check+format clean. This ticket is in_progress (assignee config-loop); USER DECISION on a621: "implement 0ac6, then close a621" — so a621 stays OPEN until this closes. DONE: slice 1/4 (ticket_clarity.threshold routed through typed Config; new ticket_clarity section + config.read_config_file() helper; gates._clarity_threshold rerouted; _read_config_key removed). REMAINING 3 slices (do one per iteration: implement -> add tests across param x location combos -> subagent code review -> remediate -> .venv/bin/python -m pytest -m "not integration and not external" -> uvx ruff check src tests + uvx ruff format -> rebar comment + commit + push). KEY DESIGN TENSION to resolve in slice 2/3: the decision chose ERGONOMIC env names that do NOT match the auto-derived REBAR_<SECTION>_<KEY> (e.g. reconciler.jira_cli_timeout <-> REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT, not REBAR_RECONCILER_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT; jira.url <-> JIRA_URL). RECOMMENDED FIX: add a per-key CANONICAL-env-name map consulted by config.env_overrides() (non-deprecated, no warning) so the nice name is the canonical override of the config-file key, plus keep the EV-3c deprecated names in _LEGACY_ENV_ALIASES (with warnings). SLICE 2 (reconciler.*): reroute acli_subprocess._acli_call_timeout (REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT; alias REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT), _advisory_lock._resolve_retry_budget (REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES; alias ..._LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET), outbound_differ deletion-probe (REBAR_RECONCILER_DELETION_PROBE_LIMIT; alias RECONCILER_ABSENT_GET_BUDGET), and rebar_id_audit._resolve_id_guard_bypass to read load_config().reconciler.* as the config-FILE layer. MUST PRESERVE: the id-guard value-flip (raise->false/warn->true), fail-CLOSED default (guard active), the LOUD per-violation bypass warning, and the legacy flat .rebar/config.conf key rebar_id_guard_mode (needs a legacy-config-key alias -> reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe with value map). SLICE 3 (jira.*): JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_PROJECT consumers (applier.py, fetcher.py, acli.py, acli_cli_ops.py, inbound_probe.py, _attestation.py) fall back to load_config().jira.* (canonical env = JIRA_URL etc.; secret JIRA_API_TOKEN stays env-only). SLICE 4 (llm.*): add [tool.rebar.llm] config-FILE support to LLMConfig.from_env (read the table via the typed loader / tomllib; env REBAR_LLM_* overrides file; keep stdlib-only/optional-extra boundary; llm is NOT in core _SECTIONS by design). HARD CONSTRAINTS throughout: stdlib-only core (tomllib; no pydantic-settings/platformdirs); PRESERVE fail-closed verify gate; typed Config + load_config is the single entry point. When all 4 slices land: update docs/config.md (move reconciler/jira/llm from the env-only section into the wired inventory; drop the "ticket 0ac6" caveats), close 0ac6, then close a621 with a completion summary.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

PROGRESS — slice 2/4 done (config-loop, hourly /loop iteration). reconciler.* now routes through the typed Config as the config-FILE layer:
- config.py: new _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES per-key map + _canonical_env_name() so the ergonomic env names are the NON-deprecated canonical overrides of the nested section key (reconciler.jira_cli_timeout<-REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT, reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe<-REBAR_UNSAFE_ID_GUARD_BYPASS); env_overrides() AND _env_signature() both consult it (cache key stays correct). EV-3c old names (REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT, REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET, RECONCILER_ABSENT_GET_BUDGET, REBAR_ID_GUARD_MODE) added to _LEGACY_ENV_ALIASES (canonical-wins + warn). _map_legacy_env extended for the REBAR_ID_GUARD_MODE value-flip (warn->true/raise->false). New _LEGACY_FLAT_CONF_KEYS + _map_legacy_flat_conf wire the legacy FLAT .rebar/config.conf key rebar_id_guard_mode -> reconciler.id_guard_bypass_unsafe (canonical dotted wins if both present).
- Consumers rerouted to load_config().reconciler.*: acli_subprocess._acli_call_timeout (jira_cli_timeout; 0/neg->120 default preserved), _advisory_lock._resolve_retry_budget (lock_max_retries; 0->max(1,0)=1; dropped now-unused os import + the two _LOCK_*_ENV consts), outbound_differ deletion-probe (deletion_probe_limit), rebar_id_audit._resolve_id_guard_bypass (id_guard_bypass_unsafe; FAIL-CLOSED on ConfigError -> guard active; loud per-violation bypass warning preserved). _get_rebar_id_guard_mode_from_config + _rebar_env retained (applier re-exports them).
- Tests: new tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_file_wiring.py (21 cases: param x location {pyproject, rebar.toml, legacy dotted+flat conf, XDG user, canonical+legacy env, rebar -c} x precedence CLI>env>project>user>default + fail-closed + provenance). Updated test_rebar_id_label_writers.py guard-mode-precedence to drive config via the real legacy conf + env (no longer patches the bypassed helper).
- Full offline suite GREEN (2016 passed, was 1995; +21), ruff check + format clean, subagent review clean (all 7 invariants confirmed: fail-closed id-guard, value-flip, canonical-env mapping, precedence, default-value parity, no dead imports, cache correctness). REMAINING: slice 3 (jira.* JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT consumers fall back to load_config().jira.*, secret JIRA_API_TOKEN env-only), slice 4 (llm.* [tool.rebar.llm] in LLMConfig.from_env).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

PROGRESS — slice 3/4 done (config-loop, hourly /loop iteration). jira.* (url/user/project) now routes through the typed Config as the config-FILE layer; secret JIRA_API_TOKEN stays env-ONLY.
- config.py: added the jira.* canonical env names to _CANONICAL_ENV_NAMES (jira.url<-JIRA_URL, jira.user<-JIRA_USER, jira.project<-JIRA_PROJECT, the Atlassian-standard unprefixed names; both env_overrides() and _env_signature() already consult _canonical_env_name so resolution + cache key agree).
- New shared resolver acli_subprocess.resolve_jira_settings(project_default="") -> JiraSettings(url, user, project, api_token): url/user/project from load_config().jira.* (env overrides file), api_token from os.environ ONLY (never a config key). On ConfigError it degrades to env-only so a malformed config never breaks a reconcile pass. project_default substitutes for an empty project (e.g. "DIG").
- Rerouted all 9 reconciler consumer sites: acli.transition_issue, acli_cli_ops.update_priority, fetcher (snapshot client + parent-map project key), applier (inbound write-back client + outbound CREATE client, project_default="DIG"), reconcile (comment client), _attestation.build_acli_client_from_env (project_default="DIG", required-check now on resolved url/user + env token), inbound_probe._resolve_env (required-check on resolved values).
- IMPORTANT test-fake fix: the resolver is read from the STABLE acli_subprocess floor, NOT the acli module returned by _load_acli() (tests swap that with a fake AcliClient-only module). Initially routing via acli_mod.resolve_jira_settings caused 60 failures; fixed by importing acli_subprocess at each site. The faked AcliClient still gets the same (empty) url/user as before, so behavior is preserved.
- Tests: new tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_config_jira_wiring.py (11 cases: param x location {pyproject, rebar.toml, legacy dotted conf, XDG user, canonical env JIRA_URL, rebar -c} x precedence CLI>env>project>user>default + project_default + secret-token-env-only + malformed->env fallback + provenance + the _attestation required-check consumer). Updated test_inbound_probe message assertion. Fixed test_config_show's autouse env-cleaner to clear CANONICAL env names + legacy aliases (an exported ambient JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER was leaking into the 'sources' provenance).
- Subagent review CLEAN on all 8 invariants (secret env-only, canonical env mapping + cache key, test-fake robustness with no remaining buggy instance, required-check preserved, project_default preserved, precedence, no unused imports/stray reads, coverage). One MINOR out-of-scope finding: bridge_fsck.py (_engine_support, NOT the reconciler, not on any live path) has a 10th AcliClient site still reading JIRA_* env directly -> filed follow-up 3b92-8ef3-661b-49b2 (discovered_from 0ac6).
- Full offline suite green; ruff check + format clean.
REMAINING: slice 4 (llm.* via [tool.rebar.llm] in LLMConfig.from_env, keeping the stdlib-only/optional-extra boundary), then docs/config.md update + close 0ac6 + close epic a621.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

PROGRESS — slice 4/4 done (config-loop, hourly /loop iteration). llm.* now has [tool.rebar.llm] config-FILE support in LLMConfig.from_env, env overrides file, stdlib-only/optional-extra boundary preserved.
- DESIGN: llm.* is deliberately NOT a core _SECTIONS key (importing rebar.llm must never pull the agents stack into the stdlib core, and core Config has no llm field). So config.py gains a RESERVED-SECTIONS mechanism: _RESERVED_SECTIONS = {"llm"}; coerce_sparse SKIPS reserved sections silently (BEFORE the unknown-section warn/error, so even REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error never rejects [tool.rebar.llm]); + two helpers read_reserved_section("llm", root) (merged user<project RAW table via the SAME core discovery, ConfigError propagated) and cli_overrides_for("llm") (the rebar -c llm.* layer). This keeps load_config the single discovery entry point WITHOUT putting llm in the typed Config.
- llm/config.py LLMConfig.from_env: reads the [tool.rebar.llm] table via the core loader and layers per key CLI (rebar -c llm.KEY=VALUE) > REBAR_LLM_<KEY> env > config file > default for the non-secret knobs: model, model_provider, base_url, max_tokens, max_steps(->max_iterations; legacy alias REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS preserved + still warns, in the env layer), timeout(->timeout_s), mcp_servers (TOML inline table OR JSON string; env JSON overrides file; malformed->{}). Helpers _read_llm_file_table (ConfigError -> {} env-only fallback so a broken core config never breaks an LLM op), _llm_str, _llm_int.
- STAYS env-only (NOT file keys): secret api_key (REBAR_LLM_API_KEY), runtime-only repo_path (REBAR_LLM_REPO_PATH), DERIVED runner (REBAR_LLM_EXPERIMENTAL_HARNESS/LANGFLOW_* logic untouched), langfuse/langflow secrets.
- Tests: new tests/unit/test_config_llm.py (13 cases: param x location {pyproject, rebar.toml [llm], legacy dotted conf, XDG user, REBAR_LLM_* env, rebar -c} x precedence CLI>env>project>user>default + legacy-alias + secret-env-only + repo_path-env-only + mcp env-JSON-overrides-file + malformed->env + the cross-cutting core-no-warn/reserved-section + llm-absent-from-show-config-sources invariants).
- Subagent review CLEAN on all 7 invariants (stdlib boundary, no spurious warn, precedence, secrets/runtime/derived env-only, malformed->env, mcp dict-vs-str, no dead code). Added the one suggested edge test (env mcp JSON overrides file). Full offline suite green (2049); ruff check + format clean.
ALL 4 SLICES LANDED. Next: docs/config.md finalization (move reconciler/jira/llm from the env-only section into the wired inventory + drop the 0ac6 caveats), then close 0ac6 + close epic a621.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

DONE — all 4 slices landed + docs finalized. Commits on main: 2f8faa9d (slice 1 ticket_clarity), 8b4d797f (slice 2 reconciler.*), 287788df (slice 3 jira.*), 48783029 (slice 4 llm.*), 25da9388 (docs/config.md). reconciler.*/jira.*/llm.* + ticket_clarity all now consume their config-FILE layer through the single load_config entry point (llm via the reserved-section read_reserved_section path, NOT core Config). Each slice: exhaustive PARAMETERS x LOCATIONS tests (precedence CLI>env>project>user>default + portability), subagent review clean, full offline suite green (final 2049), ruff clean. Slice 3 ALSO validated LIVE against the Jira DIG project (dry-run reconcile + full link round-trip passed; the one xfail is a pre-existing ACLI-version primitive limitation). Preserved throughout: fail-closed verify gate, id-guard value-flip/fail-CLOSED/loud bypass warning/legacy flat rebar_id_guard_mode key, stdlib-only core. One discovered follow-up: 3b92 (bridge_fsck out-of-scope 10th JIRA_* site). AC met; closing.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## What

Surfaced by the f81a docs sweep. The typed Config (252e) and the design-of-record (docs/config.md) present reconciler.*, jira.*, and llm.* as [tool.rebar] config keys, but their CONSUMERS still read raw env, so the config-FILE side is unwired:

- reconciler.* (jira_cli_timeout, lock_max_retries, deletion_probe_limit, id_guard_bypass_unsafe) and jira.* (url/user/project) ARE parsed into the typed Config and shown by `rebar config`, but the reconciler reads REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT / REBAR_RECONCILER_* / JIRA_URL etc. from os.environ directly — a [tool.rebar.reconciler]/[tool.rebar.jira] file value is silently ignored (footgun: rebar config shows a value that isn't used).
- llm.* is NOT a core Config section at all (it would warn as an unknown section); LLMConfig.from_env is env-only (REBAR_LLM_*), no tomllib.

The env renames + aliases (EV-3c/EV-4) are done and correct; this is the remaining config-FILE routing the original decision intended ("config-ify under reconciler.*", "[tool.rebar] llm.* config keys"). f81a documents the current env-only reality; this ticket closes the gap.
## Scope / Deliverables

- Route the reconciler tunable reads + the jira.* reads through load_config().reconciler/.jira as the config-FILE layer, with the existing "nice" env names kept as the env override (resolve the env-name-vs-section-key mismatch, e.g. REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT overriding reconciler.jira_cli_timeout); preserve the id-guard value-flip + fail directions.
- Add config-file support to the rebar.llm layer for llm.* (a [tool.rebar.llm] table read via tomllib in LLMConfig, env overriding file), keeping the stdlib-only/optional-extra boundary.
- Update docs/config.md to move these from "env-only" back into the wired [tool.rebar] inventory once consumed.
## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A [tool.rebar.reconciler]/[tool.rebar.jira]/[tool.rebar.llm] config-file value is actually consumed (not just shown by `rebar config`), with env override precedence + tests (file x env x default)
- [ ] No regression to the env names/aliases from EV-3c/EV-4; verify gate untouched
- [ ] docs/config.md updated to reflect the now-wired keys; full non-integration suite green

content.text:
DONE — S1 implemented in src/rebar/grounding/ (commit 352d0070).

WHAT SHIPPED (all 4 ACs met):
- AC1 evidence model: evidence.py — CLOSED reason enum (version_skew/invalid_detector/other first-class),
  refuted/match/abstain in ONE shape, coverage record; SARIF map BOTH directions (sarif.py); canonical
  grounding.schema.json is the source of truth (validated in tests).
- AC2 fail-open harness: harness.py — OUT-OF-PROCESS subprocess reaper (mirrors acli_subprocess: process-group
  SIGTERM→grace→SIGKILL→bounded drain, ESRCH/EPERM-guarded, pipe-holding-grandchild reaped) AND IN-PROCESS
  worker-subprocess (survives a hung worker AND a hard signal-death standing in for a segfaulting C-extension).
  Missing tool / timeout / crash / version-skew / spawn-OSError → recorded abstain, never raises.
- AC3 optional extra: [grounding] (tree-sitter) in pyproject + _optional registration; core import-clean
  (tree_sitter in the optionality _HEAVY gate; `import rebar.grounding` asserted leak-free).
- AC4 unit-tested: tests/unit/grounding/ — each failure mode → correct abstain(reason)+coverage. 42 grounding
  tests + the optionality + schema-coverage guards pass; mypy clean; all files < 800 LOC (largest 414).

CODE REVIEW (per epic policy, a subagent reviewed all S1 work before close):
- Found + FIXED: BLOCKER B1 (large worker result deadlocked the pipe → was misreported as timeout; now drained
  concurrently via poll/recv), MAJOR M1 (spawn OSError could raise → now fail-open abstain), MAJOR M2 (per-call
  Process FD leak → proc.close() in finally), MAJOR M3 (worker version-skew parity with run_tool added), plus
  minors (run_tool communicate OSError guard; untrusted-SARIF-bag trust flag; parse_error detail wording).
- Regression tests added for B1/M1/M3 + the untrusted-bag path.
- Accepted-as-is: a narrow-except nit in _reap_worker and the OOM-vs-segfault detail wording (both fail-open).

CONSUMERS UNBLOCKED: S2 (1532 Engine A), S3 (2554 T0 deps), S4 (48d7 Engine B) depend_on this and are now ready.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why
This is the dependency root of the oracle. The three-valued evidence contract, the fail-open execution harness,
and the optional-extra scaffolding are shared by every engine — they must exist and be stable before Engine A,
T0, or Engine B are built. Getting the contract right here is what makes "one implementation, not two" real.
## What
- The normalized **evidence model**: `resolved | abstained(reason)` with a CLOSED structured reason enum
(`unsupported_lang | no_tool | parse_error | timeout | ambiguous | private_or_internal_suspected |
network_error | rate_limited | version_skew | invalid_detector | other`), provenance/tier, and a coverage
record. `version_skew` (the #1 real failure) and `invalid_detector` (a project detector failing validation)
are first-class; `other` is the explicit catch-all (no open `…`). Resolution-lane resolved value is `refuted`
(never an asserted absence). One internal finding shape; SARIF 2.1.0 map at the edges (superset).
- The **fail-open recover/timeout harness** every backend runs inside. OUT-OF-PROCESS tools (ctags, ast-grep,
OpenGrep, scc/lizard, registry HTTP) use the subprocess reaper (mirror `acli_subprocess`: process-group,
SIGTERM→SIGKILL, structured timeout). IN-PROCESS bindings (tree-sitter) CANNOT be bounded by a thread/signal
timeout (it can't interrupt a hung C-extension call, and a segfault would kill the host) — so prefer the CLI
form of every matcher, and run any unavoidable in-process binding inside a WORKER SUBPROCESS so the same reaper
applies. A crash/timeout/missing-tool/version-skew becomes a recorded `abstain`, never raises; the visible skip
IS the coverage record — never a silent no-op. Each backend's version is pinned/recorded.
- **Optional-extra scaffolding**: a new extra in `pyproject.toml`, `_optional.py` guard + `guard_import`, core
stays import-clean (the existing optionality CI gate), non-adopting clients import nothing.
## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/` (new package) — contract + harness + extra wiring only; no backend logic.
- Polyglot/optional by construction; no Python-specific assumptions in the contract.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evidence model + CLOSED structured reason enum (incl. version_skew, invalid_detector, other) + coverage implemented; `refute`/`abstain` representable; SARIF map both directions.

- [ ] Fail-open harness covers OUT-OF-PROCESS (subprocess reaper) AND IN-PROCESS (worker-subprocess) backends: crash / hang / timeout / missing tool / version-skew → recorded `abstain`, never raises — incl. a segfaulting C-extension parse.
- [ ] Optional extra added; core import-clean (optionality CI gate passes); non-adopting client imports nothing.
- [ ] Unit-tested: harness converts each failure mode to the correct `abstain(reason)` + coverage entry.

content.text:
Implemented on main (commit 1dbe5e7e).

Factored the canonical serializer into a new lock-free, dependency-free module
`rebar._store.canonical` (`canonical_str` / `canonical_bytes`) and routed every
live event writer through it; `event_append.py` now imports it (its private
`_canonical_bytes` removed) and its false docstring (citing a never-created .sh
test) is corrected.

Writers conformed (were emitting unsorted json.dumps):
  - _commands/txn.py (STATUS + claim STATUS/EDIT), _commands/delete.py (UNLINK
    cascade + STATUS(deleted)/ARCHIVED), _commands/compact.py (SNAPSHOT),
    graph/_links.py (LINK), rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py (inbound SYNC),
    rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py (BRIDGE_ALERT).

Tests (all green; default suite 2055 + integration 19 + these):
  - tests/unit/test_canonical.py — byte contract + >2^53-int & non-ASCII fuzz +
    the stdlib structural guard (AST/regex scan of src/rebar, raw-serializer set
    asserted EMPTY).
  - tests/interfaces/store/test_canonical_event_bytes.py — cross-producer parity:
    every committed event file == canonical_bytes(its own parsed content).
  - tests/interfaces/store/test_e3_txn_bytes.py — flipped from pinning the OLD
    unsorted/spaced txn bytes to asserting the canonical form (its own TODO marked
    this as P1.0 scope).

DEVIATION from the epic (codebase moved on since it was written): the four bash
heredoc writers (ticket-comment/revert/lib-api/compact.sh) and the dead leaf
scripts (ticket-create/edit.sh) were ALREADY removed by the bash→Python
strangler-fig migration — there are no bash event writers in src/. So AC items
"4 bash heredoc writers call python3 -m rebar._store.canonical" and "dead leaf
writers deleted" are satisfied/moot; no `python3 -m rebar._store.canonical` CLI
was added (no bash caller exists). The structural guard keeps its (now-inert)
bash prong as a regression backstop. The parity test is a pytest module, not the
AC's `.sh` name, because tests/scripts is now a pytest tier.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
# P1.0 — Unify canonical event-byte serialization + zero-dep structural guard
**Prerequisite for P2.1, P2.2, P2.3.** Every later item that adds, reorders, or
signs event content depends on there being exactly ONE canonical byte format.
## Context (problem + research)
Event serialization is scattered across many writers and only **one** is
canonical — `_store/event_append.py:56-61` emits
`json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False)`. Seven
other LIVE writers emit plain `json.dumps(event, ensure_ascii=False)` (unsorted):
reconciler `_engine/event_append.py:123`, `ticket_txn.py:219/351/372`,
`graph/_links.py:145`, `ticket-delete-unlink-scan.py:149`,
`batch_dispatch.py:222`; plus bash heredocs in `ticket-comment.sh:90-91`
(live via `ticket-transition.sh:439` force-close), `ticket-revert.sh:170-174`
(live via `ticket-transition.sh:179` un-archive), `ticket-lib-api.sh:994/1017`
(delete), `ticket-compact.sh:274` (SNAPSHOT). There is **no** byte-parity test —
the one cited in `_store/event_append.py:15`'s docstring does not exist.
**Research / proven art.** The "`json.dumps == jq -S -c`" assumption is unsafe:
`jq` parses numbers as IEEE-754 float64, and rebar's ns `timestamp` is a 19-digit
integer **> 2^53** — jq <=1.6 (default macOS) rounds it on parse, and even jq-1.7
rounds it under arithmetic. The TUF/sigstore ecosystem's hard-won lesson is
"canonicalization across implementations is a footgun; standardize on one / sign
the literal bytes" (RFC 8785 JCS; python-tuf#457; sigstore DSSE). So: collapse to
ONE Python serializer, keep jq out of the event path entirely.
## Evidence (experiments)
- EXP2: `json.dumps(...ensure_ascii=False)` == `jq -S -c` ONLY with
`ensure_ascii=False` (`世界` diverges otherwise).
- EXP-jq: jq rounds the >2^53 timestamp (`.t+0` -> `...655000`); confirmed.
- EXP-R3 (real code): real `_canonical_bytes` != plain dumps, but parsed dicts are
identical -> **re-serialization is replay-safe** (reducer reads keys, not bytes).
- EXP-R7 (real code): `python3 -m rebar._store.<submodule>` works and emits
canonical sorted output -> bash heredocs can call the helper.
- EXP-R9/R9b (real code): the committed stdlib guard
`docs/experiments/event_write_guard.py` flags **exactly 7 Python + 7 bash** event writers
with **0 false positives** across 73 read/output `json.dumps` sites.
## What / Implementation detail
1. Factor a **lock-free** `canonical_bytes(event) -> bytes` helper (out of
`_store/event_append.py:56-61`) importable without the write lock (the txn/LINK
paths rename+commit inline, e.g. `ticket_txn.py:236-243`).
2. Route every live writer through it: the 5 Python sites import it; the 4 bash
heredocs call `python3 -m rebar._store.canonical` (pass the event(s) on stdin;
**batch** multi-event paths — delete writes STATUS+ARCHIVED+N*UNLINK — in ONE
invocation to avoid per-event subprocess cost).
3. Delete the dead leaf writers (`ticket-create.sh`, `ticket-edit.sh`) and leave
`ticket-link.sh` (still serves `link --dry-run`, writes no event) /
`ticket-migrate-*.sh` (one-shot) exempt. Fix the false docstring at
`_store/event_append.py:15`.
4. Ship the **stdlib** structural guard as a CI test (semgrep/ast-grep/pre-commit
are ABSENT here — EXP-R9). Python prong = AST (json.dump(s) on a dict carrying
`event_type`); bash prong = regex scoped to `json.dump` co-occurring with an
`'event_type'` dict (a bare grep false-positives on ~10 read/output `.sh`).
## Success Criteria
- All event files (every type) are byte-identical regardless of writer, pinned by
a Python<->Python parity test (incl. a >2^53 int + non-ASCII fuzz case).
- The structural guard fails CI on any new raw event serializer; after this epic
its expected writer set is **empty** (all routed through the helper).
- No reducer/replay behavior change (existing committed data unaffected;
`*.retired` bytes are read, not re-serialized).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `canonical_bytes()` extracted as a lock-free helper and imported by the 5 Python writers
- [ ] 4 bash heredoc writers call `python3 -m rebar._store.canonical` (multi-event paths batched)
- [ ] dead leaf writers deleted; `_store/event_append.py:15` docstring corrected
- [ ] `tests/scripts/test-ticket-write-commit-event.sh` added (parity + >2^53/non-ASCII fuzz)
- [ ] stdlib structural guard wired into CI (Python AST + scoped bash regex), expected writer set empty
- [ ] reconciler + compaction suites still green (no replay change)
See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.0) for the full write-up and `docs/experiments/` for the prototypes.

REB-19.summary: P1.0 — Unify canonical event-byte serialization + zero-dep structural guard

content.text:
Realigned to the SHIPPED code-grounding oracle (prereq epic 8f6c, now CLOSED): P2/P3/P4 consume the src/rebar/grounding/ facade (refute_absence/applies/scan) — universal-ctags (T1 resolution), deps.dev+syft (T0 deps), scc/lizard (size) — NOT new tool integrations. Corrected the stale 'LSP via solidlsp' for P2 (ctags is the v1 resolver; solidlsp/LSP is the T2 follow-on epic 850f). Fail-open is the oracle's abstain-by-default contract. See epic 5fd2 prerequisite-foundations update.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py: P1-P8 implemented, polyglot + fail-open (each check abstains on error, never aborts the floor). P1 readiness-shape (BLOCKS), P2/P3 consume the 8f6c grounding oracle (refute_absence) for file/package resolution (coverage-only, never block), P4 oversize, P5 task-DAG cycle (BLOCKS) + file interference (advisory), P6 AC quality, P7 destructive sniff, P8 reviewability/context-budget (BLOCKS). exec=DET tier dispatched by the orchestrator's code executor. Coverage recorded via det_coverage() onto the attestation. DET floor is the only tier that blocks by default in v1. Unit-tested: tests/unit/test_plan_review.py (P1/P5-cycle/P7/P8 + fail-open + P2/P3-never-block).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

**Completion verification — PASS**

All acceptance criteria are met. Evidence grounded in the repository:

**AC1 — P1–P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool**
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py` implements all eight checks: `p1_readiness_shape` (lines 141–169), `p2_resolution` (211–253), `p3_package_existence` (263–296), `p4_oversize` (305–337), `p5_task_dag` (341–398), `p6_ac_quality` (487–530), `p7_destructive` (547–576), `p8_reviewability` (580–623).
- `run_det_floor()` (line 639) wraps every check in `try/except` → `abstain` record on error, never aborts the floor — ironclad fail-open.
- `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py` covers P1 block/pass, P5 cycle-block/leaf/interference-advisory, P7 destructive with/without safeguard, P8 block/pass, fail-open monkeypatch, and P2/P3 never-block + abstain-without-repo.

**AC2 — Coverage recorded on the attestation**
- Orchestrator calls `det_floor.det_coverage(det_results)` and stores it in `coverage["det"]` (orchestrator.py line 259), which flows into the returned `plan_review_verdict` dict. The `det_coverage()` function (det_floor.py line 678) emits per-check ran/abstain/status records.

**AC3 — DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1**
- `pass3_decide` (passes.py line 390) defaults `blocking_enabled=False` making every LLM-tier criterion advisory by default; blocking requires explicit opt-in per criterion (`default_posture: "advisory"` in criteria_v8.json, e.g. line 39).
- Only DET checks P1, P5-cycle, and P8 carry `blocking=True` findings.

**AC4 (added) — P8 reviewability DET check**
- `p8_reviewability` (det_floor.py line 580) computes a token estimate and fails with "ticket too big to review in full; reduce/decompose it" when content (or largest parent+child pairing for containers) exceeds the largest configured context window, even at one-criterion-per-call.
- Tests `test_p8_blocks_when_too_big` and `test_p8_passes_for_normal_plan` confirm both branches.

**AC5 (added) — P1–P8 run as deterministic exec=DET tier dispatched by orchestrator (no LLM)**
- `orchestrator.py` line 255: `# ── DET tier (exec=DET) ──────` labels the first-class execution path.
- Lines 256–259: `det_floor.run_det_floor(ctx)` is called before any LLM pass and with no LLM involvement.
- P8-too-big short-circuits the entire LLM tier (line 263–267).

**Verdict: PASS** — every stated acceptance criterion is demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

CONTAINER REVIEW — G3 (child coverage) of parent epic `5fd2` × child `012e` (Layer-1 DET floor). Net: this child FULLY covers the parent's Layer-1 DET slice; the union with its siblings leaves no DET parent criterion uncovered. Two coverage/consistency findings (both minor, neither an uncovered-parent-criterion gap), then the affirmed coverage map.

═══ FINDING 1 — Parent SC#2 "coverage recorded ON THE ATTESTATION" is split across `012e` + `4bb7`; the child OWNS coverage-record CONTENT but not attestation-binding — confirm the seam is closed ═══
- criteria: [G3]
- location: child `012e` AC#2 "Coverage recorded on the attestation"; parent `5fd2` Success-Criterion #2 ("...with coverage recorded on the attestation") and AC#5 ("Attestation: ...coverage record").
- evidence:
  - The child's AC#2 verbatim says "Coverage recorded on the attestation" — but the child's actual deliverable (per its implementation comment) is `det_floor.det_coverage(det_results)` producing per-check ran/abstain/status records. The orchestrator (`ca03`) stores that into `coverage["det"]`, and the ATTESTATION SIGNING that binds coverage into the signed manifest lives in sibling `4bb7` (`attest.build_manifest` signs the coverage counts).
  - So the parent's single "coverage recorded on the attestation" criterion is covered by a THREE-party chain: `012e` (mints the DET coverage payload) → `ca03` (assembles it into the verdict coverage dict) → `4bb7` (binds coverage into the signed manifest). This is correct decomposition, NOT an uncovered parent criterion — I checked the roster and `4bb7` demonstrably covers the attestation-binding half (its verify comment: "Coverage: ...recorded in the schema and the signed manifest").
  - The container-coverage RISK is only the seam wording: the child's AC#2 reads as if `012e` itself records coverage *on the attestation*, but `012e` has no attestation code — it emits a coverage structure a sibling signs. If `012e`'s `det_coverage()` shape and the field `4bb7` actually signs ever drift, the parent SC#2 ("coverage recorded on the attestation") could be partially-covered (DET coverage computed but not the part that lands in the signed manifest) without either child's own AC failing.
- scenarios: A future edit changes `det_coverage()` to add a per-check key that `ca03`/`4bb7` don't propagate into the signed `coverage` block → the attestation's coverage record silently omits part of the DET floor; both children still pass their own ACs.
- impact: Low — both halves are implemented and the completion-verifier confirmed each. This is a wording/seam-ownership clarification, not a gap.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the DET-coverage→signed-manifest seam: `012e.det_coverage()` output is the SAME structure `4bb7` binds into the signed attestation (no field dropped between `ca03` assembly and `4bb7` signing), so parent SC#2 "coverage recorded ON THE ATTESTATION" is end-to-end covered, not just "DET coverage computed."
- suggested_fix: Reword `012e` AC#2 to its true scope — "DET-floor coverage (per-check ran/abstain/status) is EMITTED for the attestation to record" — and note the binding is `4bb7`'s; or add a cross-child test asserting the det_coverage keys survive into the signed manifest.

═══ FINDING 2 — Title/What "(P1-P7)" undercounts P8; the parent's P8 reviewability criterion IS covered here but the child header could read as excluding it ═══
- criteria: [G3]
- location: child `012e` TITLE "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7)..." and its "## What" line (enumerates "P1...P7"); the P8 criterion lives only in the later "(Added) P8" section. Parent `5fd2` AC#1 explicitly lists "...and P8 reviewability/context-budget" as part of the SAME Layer-1 DET criterion, and P8 also appears in the parent's P8 container-pairing requirement.
- evidence:
  - The parent's Layer-1 AC and registry treat P8 as an integral DET check ("P1–P8" in the parent's T8-DET-execution-path AC and the registry "P8 reviewability/context-budget"). The child DOES cover P8 — it has a dedicated "(Added) P8" AC and the implementation includes `p8_reviewability` (blocks when too big) — so this is FULLY covered, NOT an absence.
  - The container-coverage concern is purely that the child's TITLE and primary "What" enumeration say "P1-P7", so a reader auditing "does a child own the parent's P8 criterion?" against the header alone would mis-read P8 as out-of-scope. P8 is also the criterion that carries the CONTAINER-specific clause ("a parent+child pairing too big to review in full") — i.e. it is load-bearing for G3/G4 themselves — so its visibility in the owning child matters more than for an ordinary sub-check.
- scenarios: A registry-coverage or decomposition audit keying on the child title concludes "P8 has no owning child" and either duplicates it into `ca03` or files it as an uncovered parent criterion — when it is in fact fully implemented in `012e`.
- impact: Low — purely a header/enumeration mismatch; the AC and code cover P8. Flagged so the title doesn't mislead the next coverage audit.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Make P8 visible in the child's primary identity: update the title/What enumeration to "P1-P8" (or "P1-P7 + P8 reviewability") so a coverage audit keying on the header sees P8's owner, matching the parent's "P1–P8" framing.
- suggested_fix: Retitle to "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8) + fail-open polyglot tool wrappers" and fold the P8 bullet into the main "## What" list.

═══ AFFIRMED COVERAGE MAP (4-bucket, parent criteria this child is the natural owner of) ═══
- Parent AC#1 (Layer-1 DET: readiness-shape P1, resolution P2, package P3, oversize P4, task-DAG P5, AC/DD-quality P6, destructive P7, reviewability P8; Python, polyglot, fail-open skipped=pass) → FULLY covered (item-for-item match; impl `det_floor.py` P1–P8, oracle-consumed P2/P3/P4, fail-open via abstain).
- Parent T8-DET-execution-path AC ("P1–P8 run as exec=DET via the code executor") → FULLY covered (child's added AC + `run_det_floor` dispatched by `ca03`'s code executor).
- Parent SC#2 ("DET floor blocks only on sound checks, fails open, coverage recorded") → block-soundness + fail-open FULLY covered here; coverage-recorded half is PARTIAL-at-this-child-by-design (see Finding 1 — the attestation-binding half is `4bb7`'s, which DOES cover it; no roster gap).
- All OTHER parent ACs/SCs (Pass-1/2/3, attestation, claim, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar, Pass-4 coach, priority-cap, eval-suite, config, docs, bugs-exempt) → NOT this child's slice; each is owned by a dedicated sibling (1913/acc1/487d/4bb7/092b/db7b/75a9/55de/7284/6c5c/3e50). Cross-checked against the full roster — no DET parent criterion is left uncovered by the union of children.

No uncovered-parent-criterion (absence) finding: every Layer-1/DET parent criterion is covered by this child, and every non-DET parent criterion is covered by a named sibling. The two findings above are seam-wording/header-enumeration consistency items, not coverage gaps.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

CONTAINER REVIEW — G4 (child consistency) of parent epic `5fd2` × child `012e` (Layer-1 DET floor). Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the WHOLE sibling roster. NET: no contradiction, no scope-overlap collision, no ordering/dependency gap that breaks the shipped slice. Two LOW residual-reference / implicit-shared-state consistency findings (both wording/seam, not contradictions). Modes that are CLEAN are affirmed at the end.

═══ FINDING 1 (mode: IMPLICIT SHARED STATE + CONSUMER IMPACT) — the DET-coverage payload (`det_coverage()`) is an implicitly-shared data structure flowing 012e → ca03 → 4bb7 with NO pinned cross-child contract ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `012e` "## Scope"/AC#2 ("Coverage recorded on the attestation"; impl `det_floor.det_coverage()` emitting per-check ran/abstain/status); sibling `ca03` (stores it into `coverage["det"]`, orchestrator.py); sibling `4bb7` (`attest.build_manifest` binds the coverage counts into the HMAC-signed manifest).
- evidence:
  - 012e MINTS a coverage structure (per-check ran/abstain/status), ca03 ASSEMBLES it into the verdict `coverage` dict, and 4bb7 SIGNS coverage into the manifest. Three children share one undocumented data shape across the seam; no child declares the schema as a contract the others depend on (012e `deps: []`; ca03/4bb7 reference it only in prose + implementation).
  - The G3 container review on this same pairing already independently flagged the seam-drift risk (Finding 1 there): a future edit adding a `det_coverage()` key that ca03/4bb7 don't propagate would silently drop part of the DET coverage from the SIGNED attestation while every child still passes its own AC. That is precisely an implicit-shared-state consistency hazard for G4.
  - Roster cross-check: this is NOT an uncovered-criterion absence — both halves ship (012e emits, 4bb7 signs, verify-PASS on both). It is a CONSISTENCY note: the shared payload has no contract test spanning the three children, so the coupling is implicit.
- scenarios: `det_coverage()` gains/renames a per-check field; ca03 forwards only the old keys; 4bb7 signs the truncated set → the attestation's "coverage recorded" (parent SC#2) silently omits part of the DET floor, undetected by any single child's tests.
- impact: Low — all three halves are implemented and individually verify-PASS; the risk is future drift across an implicit shared structure, not a present contradiction.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the DET-coverage payload as an explicit cross-child contract: add a shared test (or schema) asserting the keys 012e's `det_coverage()` emits survive ca03's assembly into `coverage["det"]` AND land in the field 4bb7 binds into the signed manifest — so the implicit 012e→ca03→4bb7 shared state cannot silently drift.
- suggested_fix: Add a cross-child invariant test ("det_coverage keys round-trip into the signed manifest"); optionally lift the coverage payload into the `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` so all three children share one declared shape.

═══ FINDING 2 (mode: RESIDUAL REFERENCES) — child header/What say "(P1-P7)" but the child ships P8, and P7's "escalates T4" resolves only to a suggested_fix STRING, not a wired escalation — both leave stale references a sibling/audit can mis-read ═══
- criteria: [G4]
- location: child `012e` TITLE "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P7)..." + the "## What" enumeration ("P1...P7"); P8 lives only in the later "(Added) P8" section. Separately, "## What": "P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates T4)" and `det_floor.py:p7_destructive` (suggested_fix mentions T4); the actual T4 overlay is owned/fired by sibling `b711` (LLM-routed), with `plan_review_T4.md` shipped there.
- evidence:
  - P8 enumeration: the parent treats P8 as integral DET ("P1–P8" in the parent's T8-DET-execution-path AC + the registry "P8 reviewability/context-budget"), and `ca03`'s added AC dispatches "the P1–P8 floor". The child DOES implement P8 (`p8_reviewability`, BLOCKS) — so P8 is fully covered — but the child's primary identity (title + What) still says "P1-P7". A coverage/decomposition audit keying on the header would mis-read P8 as unowned. This is a residual stale reference, not a gap. (The G3 review on this pairing flagged the same header mismatch as a coverage-audit hazard.)
  - P7→T4 "escalation": `det_floor.py:p7_destructive` does NOT wire any T4 escalation — it emits an advisory finding whose `suggested_fix` text merely mentions "(T4)"; there is no orchestrator hook (grep `P7` in orchestrator.py → none) that forces the T4 overlay to fire when P7 hits. T4 is instead fired independently by `b711`'s overlay router. So "escalates T4" is a narrative claim that maps to a string, not a mechanism. T4 is genuinely COVERED (by b711, `plan_review_T4.md` ships) — so again NOT an absence — but the "escalates" wording is a residual reference implying a cross-child mechanism that doesn't exist.
- scenarios: (a) a registry-coverage audit keyed on the child title files P8 as an uncovered parent criterion or duplicates it into ca03; (b) an implementer reads "P7 escalates T4" and expects the DET floor to trigger the T4 overlay, when in fact T4's firing is owned wholly by b711's router independent of P7.
- impact: Low — both referents are actually implemented/owned (P8 in this child; T4 in b711); purely header/enumeration + narrative-wording residue that can mislead the next audit or implementer.
- checklist_item: - [ ] Retitle to "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8)..." and fold P8 into the main "## What" list so the child's primary identity matches the parent's "P1–P8" framing; AND reword "P7 ... (escalates T4)" to its true behavior ("P7 surfaces an advisory destructive finding; the T4 compat/destructiveness overlay is fired independently by b711's router — P7 does not mechanically escalate it") so no sibling/implementer expects a P7→T4 wiring that isn't there.
- suggested_fix: Title → "Layer-1 deterministic floor (P1-P8) + fail-open polyglot tool wrappers"; change the P7 What-line to "P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (advisory; the related T4 overlay is owned/fired by `b711`, not escalated from P7)".

═══ AFFIRMED-CLEAN modes (no finding) ═══
- CONFLICTING ASSUMPTIONS — CLEAN. 012e AC#3 "DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1; per-criterion LLM blocking is OPT-IN" is CONSISTENT with `487d` (Pass-3: `blocking_enabled=False` default, per-criterion `block_threshold` 0.95 opt-in) and with the parent design summary ("Defaults ship advisory; the DET floor blocks structurally"). 012e's only blocking checks (P1, P5-cycle, P8) align with the parent's "minimal sound DET floor is the only hard blocker". No contradiction.
- SCOPE OVERLAP — CLEAN (disambiguated). 012e's P8 (static pre-check: content / largest parent+child pairing exceeds the largest window → BLOCK) and ca03's runtime size-handling ladder (batch → one-criterion/call → escalate-model → too-big FAILURE FINDING) both reason about "too big", but the parent + both children EXPLICITLY disambiguate them (P8 = the static budget pre-check; ca03 = the dynamic context-window ladder), and ca03's verify notes "P8 remains the static pre-run budget check; this adds the dynamic fallback". No double-ownership collision. (Also: the container P8 parent+child-pairing clause is load-bearing for G3/G4 themselves and correctly lives in the DET owner.)
- DEPENDENCY GAP / ORDERING — CLEAN for the shipped slice. 012e consumes the 8f6c grounding oracle (prereq epic, CLOSED) per the parent prerequisite-foundations section; ca03 dispatches `run_det_floor` via the exec=DET code executor (T8 path, verify-PASS); 4bb7 signs the coverage 012e emits. The 012e→ca03→4bb7 chain has no declared `depends_on` edges (012e `deps: []`), but all three are closed + implemented + individually verify-PASS, so there is no runtime ordering gap. (The implicit-coupling RISK from the missing contract is captured as Finding 1, not as an ordering violation.)
- RESIDUAL REFERENCES (P2/P3/P4 → oracle) — CLEAN. 012e's "consume the 8f6c facade, do NOT re-build" is consistent with the parent's "Layer-1 CONSUMES this oracle — it no longer adds solidlsp/ast-grep/syft/scc/lizard as new integrations"; the stale "LSP via solidlsp" was already corrected to ctags (T1) in 012e's realignment comment, with solidlsp deferred to T2/`850f`. No residual contradiction.

NET: no G4 contradiction / scope-collision / ordering-gap that breaks the slice. Two LOW residual-reference / implicit-shared-state consistency items (DET-coverage payload contract; "(P1-P7)" title + "escalates T4" wording) — both referents are actually implemented/owned by a named child, so neither is an absence; they are drift/mis-read hazards to tidy, not coverage gaps.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why
The DET floor is the ONLY blocking tier (every LLM criterion is advisory in v1). It must be a frozen,
deterministic, polyglot floor that fails open on any unsupported stack.
## What
- P1 readiness-shape; P2 file/symbol/import resolution (universal-ctags via the 8f6c oracle — LSP/solidlsp is the T2 follow-on `850f`, NOT v1); P3 package existence
(deps.dev registry + optional syft SBOM); P4 oversize (scc/lizard metrics); P5 task-DAG validity + interference;

P6 AC/DD quality; P7 destructive/irreversible sniff (escalates T4). The polyglot resolution/dependency/size tooling (P2/P3/P4) is CONSUMED — via the SHIPPED `refute_absence`/`applies`/`scan` facade — from the now-CLOSED prerequisite epic 8f6c-6008-33f3-4f82 (code-grounding oracle, `src/rebar/grounding/`) — this child does NOT re-build it; it owns the rebar-specific checks (P1/P5/P6/P7) + wiring P2/P3/P4 into the DET floor.
## Scope

- Call the 8f6c grounding-oracle facade (refute_absence/applies/scan); its three-valued abstain-by-default contract IS the fail-open guarantee — unsupported lang / no tool / parse-error / timeout -> abstain (recorded reason) -> skipped = PASS (ironclad).
- The shared oracle SHIPPED as prerequisite epic 8f6c (CLOSED; also consumed by code-review `9da1`); this child depends_on it and consumes it as a library, not as new integrations.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] P1-P7 implemented in Python, polyglot, fail-open (skipped=pass) tested per tool.
- [ ] Coverage recorded on the attestation.

- [ ] DET floor is the only tier that blocks BY DEFAULT in v1 (every LLM criterion is advisory; per-criterion LLM-tier blocking is OPT-IN via its `block_threshold` — 'DET-only' is the v1 default posture, not an architectural limit).
## (Added) P8 — reviewability / context-budget (DET)
A meta-finding, not tied to any one criterion, so it lives in the deterministic floor as P8: a DET check that
PASSES unless the ticket content (or a parent+child pairing for container criteria) EXCEEDS — or is
anticipated (token-estimate) to exceed — the largest configured context window even at one-criterion-per-call.
In that case it FAILS with 'ticket too big to review in full; reduce/decompose it' (the extreme of P4 oversize
/ G5 decomposition). Deterministic (a size/estimate check, no LLM); the orchestrator emits it during the
size-handling ladder.
## Acceptance Criteria (added)

- [ ] P8 reviewability DET check: passes unless content (or a parent+child pairing) exceeds — or is anticipated to exceed — the largest context window at one-criterion-per-call; else a 'ticket too big, reduce it' failure finding.
## Acceptance Criteria (added — T8 DET execution path)
- [ ] P1–P8 run as the deterministic `exec=DET` tier the orchestrator dispatches via its code executor (no LLM) — an explicit, first-class execution path, not merely a named tier.
