content.text:
INVESTIGATION (2026-06-10, attempted, deferred): the three ACs split by coupling.

ACHIEVABLE STANDALONE:
- AC1 'no reconciler module >800 LOC' — applier.py (~3,480 LOC) can be split into seam-aligned siblings (outbound_apply / inbound_apply / apply_runtime / event_io; _build_leaves() ~:1396 is the registry seam) loaded via the SAME importlib pattern already in use. Move-only, reconciler unit suite as the gate.

COUPLED TO fare-rant-clasp (the repackage) — cannot be met without it:
- AC2 'zero importlib-by-path loads of acli' + 'delete all 5 _load_*_module shims'.
  Root cause verified in applier.py:37-39: the reconciler modules are spec-loaded BY PATH under test ('rebar_reconciler' is a test-package shadow), so normal cross-module imports between reconciler files do not resolve at test time. The five importlib-by-path loaders (mutation, _errors, adf, ticket_reducer, acli-integration) are the workaround for THAT, not only for acli-integration.py's hyphen. Renaming acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py removes the hyphen reason but the test-shadow reason remains, so the loaders are still required. Worse: splitting applier into more siblings under the current scheme needs MORE importlib loaders, not fewer.
  => 'zero importlib-by-path loads' requires the engine to be a properly importable package first — i.e. fare-rant-clasp (Rec 5 / repackage). The acli rename also has a 37-file reference blast radius (engine + tests + dispatcher) that the repackage handles holistically.

RECOMMENDATION: do fare-rant-clasp first (it makes the package importable and removes the shadow), THEN this split lands cleanly and meets all three ACs in move-only commits. Or land AC1 (size split, importlib pattern retained) standalone now and re-scope AC2 onto fare-rant-clasp. Ticket left OPEN (claim released).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

PROGRESS (tangly-abbey-smelt, 10 commits landed on main, all reconciler-unit-green):

AC1 (applier split) — COMPLETE. applier.py 3,498 -> 776 LOC across 10 cohesive, contract-named modules, none >800, none <100:
  inbound_translate (Jira->local translation+event IO), pass_io (mapping/pass-record/reschedule contract), rebar_id_audit (label-write guard), apply_base (ApplyResult+mutation/_errors loaders+_direction_guard), batch_dispatch (dispatchers+retry), apply_outbound (5 outbound leaves), apply_inbound (8 inbound leaves), typed_dispatch (_LEAVES registry+_apply_typed), apply_planning (mode caps/suppression/manifest), applier (facade+orchestrator).
  Design: clean ownership + point-of-use monkeypatch repointing (no _applier back-ref); one-directional import DAG; applier re-exports leaves for reconcile.py's getattr table. Fixed a latent _errors class-identity fork (load-once via canonical sys.modules key).

AC2 (acli rename) — COMPLETE. git mv acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py; all importlib-by-path acli loads replaced with 'from rebar_reconciler import acli' (4 engine loaders, 2 standalone scripts, 21 heredocs across the 2 e2e probes, test chokepoints). Probe test mock reworked to sys.modules+package-attr injection. Zero functional by-path acli loads remain.

REMAINING — AC1 (acli split): acli.py is still 2,178 LOC, must split (jira_fields / acli_subprocess / acli_cli_ops / AcliRestMixin / AcliGraphMixin + AcliClient core). The 1,168-LOC AcliClient mixin decomposition is the most delicate extraction (cross-mixin self. calls, MRO) and needs the [P0] real-AcliClient cross-mixin binding test added first. Then final gates: full suite (tests/unit+interfaces+scripts), wc -l bounds, rebar reconcile dry-run smoke, and the before/after live-DIG parity probes (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

COMPLETED — AC1 (acli split) done; ticket fully satisfied (all 3 ACs).

acli.py split into cohesive seam-aligned modules (move-only, commit-per-module, reconciler-unit + bridge suite green after each; baseline 926 -> 939 with the new [P0] test):
  - jira_fields.py (172) — sanitizers (_sanitize_label/summary/comment, InvalidLabelError) + local<->Jira value maps + char limits
  - acli_subprocess.py (244) — transport floor: _build_env/_check_mutation_failure/_call_with_backoff/_run_acli + typed errors (Assignee/RetryExhausted/AcliMutation) + constants
  - acli_cli_ops.py (529) — module-level CLI ops: create_issue + _create_* helpers, update_priority, get_issue, add_comment, get_comments, _parse_acli_comments, _verify_created_issue, _extract_parent_key, _attach_parent_guarded
  - acli_rest.py (274) — AcliRestMixin: _rest_urlopen_with_retry + _direct_rest_{get,put,put_raw,post_raw,post_json,delete} + issue/entity property get/set
  - acli_graph.py (489) — AcliGraphMixin: labels(+impls), links (set_relationship/get_issue_links/delete_issue_link/get_issue_link_types), parent/comment maps, comment update/delete, priority/issuetype edits
  - acli.py (677) — AcliClient(AcliRestMixin, AcliGraphMixin) core: __init__/_run/create/update/get/search/server-info/transitions/assignee/delete/set_parent; resident free transition_issue/update_issue; all re-exports

Final acli cluster sizes: acli.py 677, acli_cli_ops 529, acli_graph 489, acli_rest 274, acli_subprocess 244, jira_fields 172 — all within the 100-800 module-size policy (acli.py: 2,178 -> 677). No reconciler module touched by this work is >800; no new module <100.

Design: subprocess seam reached MODULE-QUALIFIED (acli_subprocess._run_acli) in both AcliClient._run and the acli_cli_ops free funcs -> single patch point; field-coverage suite repointed point-of-use (acli_mod.acli_subprocess/acli_cli_ops). transition_issue/update_issue kept in core (AcliClient-coupled + patched by-path in characterization suites). Added the [P0] real-AcliClient cross-mixin binding test (covers the 8 zero-coverage graph methods; builds a real AcliClient, stubs only _run/urlopen, asserts each cluster dispatches into the expected seam via MRO).

Gates:
- python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler tests/scripts/bridge -q = 939 passed (green after every commit).
- Full python -m pytest tests/unit tests/scripts tests/interfaces: only pre-existing environmental failures remain (test_engine_dir library-subprocess needs pip-installed rebar; 2 bash suites push-policy/ticket-show; tests/interfaces need installed CLI/MCP) — ALL proven identical on the pre-split baseline 19f6dea5 (zero new failures from this work).
- grep 'acli-integration': 2 prose mentions only (a test comment + docs rename note); zero functional refs.
- rebar_reconciler dry-run --dry-run-enumerate: engine imports + acli loaders OK.
- LIVE-DIG before/after parity (navasage/DIG): full reconciler e2e probes are STALE vs the current rebar CLI (use 'rebar ticket <verb>', dropped pre-existing; unrelated to acli) so were not runnable. Instead ran a direct AcliClient before/after parity harness against live DIG exercising the entire reparented mixin surface (create/get/comment via cli-ops+acli, REST property/parent-map/comment-map/transitions, graph labels/links/comment-edit/priority/issuetype, search, server-info) on BOTH the monolith (19f6dea5) and the split (HEAD): 23/23 steps IDENTICAL, zero diffs, self-cleaned (0 orphan DIG issues). This is true live round-trip validation that the mixin reparenting is behavior-preserving against real Jira.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why

Rec 4 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R3). applier.py is 3,386 LOC: outbound appliers, inbound appliers, retry machinery, audit views, event IO, and five _load_*_module importlib-by-path shims (lines ~87/114/708/730/1823) that exist only because acli-integration.py has a hyphen in its name. It is past the size an agent can hold and edit confidently.
## What
Split ONLY along call-graph seams that already exist (no new files <100 LOC):

- applier.py -> outbound_apply.py / inbound_apply.py / apply_runtime.py (retry, locks, pass-records) / event_io.py (event_io is later retired by the shared event-append ticket pokey-matte-flute). _build_leaves() (~line 1396) is the registry seam.
- Rename src/rebar/_engine/acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py; delete all five importlib shims; split client/field-mapping further only if still >800 LOC.
- Move-only commits: no logic edits in the same commit as a move.
## Scope

Behavior-preserving refactor; existing reconciler unit suites (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/) are the gate. Coordinate ordering with pokey-matte-flute (event-append) and vary-ion-fry (status-via-reducer) so seams land once.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reconciler module >800 LOC (target 200-500); no new module <100 LOC
- [ ] Zero importlib-by-path loads of acli code remain
- [ ] Move-only commits; full suite green with no test-logic changes beyond import paths

content.text:
Link-sync verification complete (empirical). VERDICT: link sync is NOT wired into the reconcile pipeline in either direction; client primitives also broken live.

Test A (in-process, tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py): BOTH FAIL (desired-behavior TDD spec).
- A1 outbound: compute_outbound_mutations emits links=[] for a local 'blocks' dep. OutboundMutation.links is hardcoded [] in both create & update branches; no _diff_links; local 'deps' (shape {target_id,relation,link_uuid}) is never read by the differ.
- A2 inbound: compute_inbound_mutations emits NO link/relation change for a Jira issuelinks array. InboundMutation has no links field; _diff_jira_vs_local never inspects issuelinks.

Live probe (tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py): set_relationship FAILS live because acli 'workitem link create' rejects --json (set_relationship passes --json at acli_graph.py:437). Without --json the link create succeeds. get_issue_links also parses the wrong key: live 'link list --json' returns {"issueLinks":[{id,outwardIssueKey,typeName}]} (camelCase 'issueLinks', flat shape) but get_issue_links parses 'issuelinks' and expects the REST {type:{name},outwardIssue:{key}} shape. So both SET and GET primitives are mismatched to actual ACLI output. All probe issues cleaned up (verified gone).

ruff: clean on both new files. Tests left red (not xfx) per instruction.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

EMPIRICAL + HISTORICAL VERDICT: link sync is NOT wired into the reconciler pipeline, and the client primitives are themselves broken against live ACLI. Independently verified.

In-process (real differs): outbound on a local ticket with deps=[{relation:blocks}] emits links=[] on every mutation (deps array reaches the differ but is never diffed); inbound on a Jira snapshot with issuelinks emits only description changes (InboundMutation has no links field; fetcher never extracts issuelinks). Both TDD tests RED -> now xfail(strict=True).

Live probe: set_relationship FAILS (passes --json to 'acli jira workitem link create', rejected by current ACLI: 'unknown flag: --json'); 'link create' WITHOUT --json works. get_issue_links parses REST shape {type:{name},outwardIssue:{key}} under key 'issuelinks', but live ACLI 'link list --json' returns {issueLinks:[{id,typeName,outwardIssueKey}]} (camelCase wrapper, flat fields). All probe issues created were deleted (cleanup confirmed).

NOT a bash->python migration regression: the bash-engine deletion commit explicitly KEPT rebar_reconciler/ as Python ('Jira sync, launched as a subprocess') — the reconciler was ALWAYS Python. Pickaxe across all 269 commits: no _diff_links, no 'LINK handler', no 'link' MutationAction ever existed; OutboundMutation.links has been inert since the upstream extraction b534f47a ('Extract DSO ticket system + Jira reconciler into standalone rebar'). The vestigial links field + primitives + 'LINK handler' docstring were carried over from the pre-extraction upstream in already-unwired form. If it ever worked, that was upstream of this repo's history, unrelated to the migration.

Verification DONE. TDD specs landed (xfail): tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py (outbound+inbound), tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py (primitives). Implementation tracked in story 25ae-92e6-2927-49b6.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why

Code inspection suggests Jira link/relation sync is scaffolded (client primitives in acli_graph.py: set_relationship/get_issue_links/delete_issue_link) but NOT wired into the diff/fetch/apply pipeline (OutboundMutation.links always []; fetcher/inbound have no link refs; no link MutationAction; no caller of the primitives). The user expects links SHOULD sync. Settle it EMPIRICALLY with tests + a live probe before re-scoping story 25ae.
## What

- Integration tests (in-process, real pipeline) that drive a rebar ticket carrying a link (blocks/depends_on) through the OUTBOUND differ and assert whether a link mutation is produced; and feed a Jira snapshot containing issuelinks through the INBOUND differ and assert whether a relation change is produced.

- A live probe (gated, authorized LIVE DIG): create two Jira issues + a rebar link between their tickets, run reconcile DRY-RUN, and observe whether the dry-run PLAN contains a link mutation (non-destructive); clean up any created issues.
- Report RAW results (do not assume outcome): does outbound emit links? does inbound reflect links? does the live dry-run plan include link ops?
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Integration tests exist exercising outbound + inbound link paths through the real differs
- [ ] A gated live probe runs reconcile dry-run with a real cross-issue rebar link and records whether links appear in the plan
- [ ] Empirical verdict recorded (works / partially / not wired) with evidence
- [ ] Suite green; ruff clean

content.text: ## What
Rewrite the docs and module docstring that still assert the old
reset+reflog recovery design, replacing them with the union-recovery safety
invariant. Documentation-only; trivially green.
## The invariant to state
> After union recovery, every commit rebar cares about is ref-reachable; therefore
> stock `git gc` is safe by construction — it only ever collects truly unreachable
> objects.
## Scope (files)
- `docs/concurrency.md` (~165-196): rewrite the section that describes
`reset --hard` + reflog-based recovery to the union-merge / ref-reachability model.
- `src/rebar/_store/sync.py`: rewrite the module docstring (asserts the old design).
- Note `git maintenance run` as the scale-up posture, and that git`s own ~30d
unreachable-reflog window remains as a free backstop we no longer depend on.
## Depends on
WU-1, WU-2, WU-3 (docs describe their landed behaviour).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` rewritten to the union-recovery invariant (no stale reset+reflog claims)
- [ ] `sync.py` module docstring rewritten to match implemented behaviour
- [ ] Safety invariant stated explicitly
- [ ] `git maintenance` noted as scale-up; reflog noted as non-load-bearing backstop
- [ ] No remaining doc/docstring claim that the reflog is the recovery net

content.text:
DONE (commit cda9841). All 6 AC met. Four divergence sources found+fixed (experiment-driven, faithful stateful fake on git-backed repo): (A) bind_confirm at inbound create — outbound re-create eliminated; (B) legacy snapshot differ no longer echoes own rebar-id:/rebar-status: write-back as outbound update (suppression at source; the two strict-xfail e2/e3 cases now pass for real); (C) status reverse-map was lexicographic inversion — imported 'In Progress'->blocked and 'Done'->cancelled (DATA BUG beyond idempotency); now canonical config.jira_to_local_status with inbound_differ parity test, rebar-status: label precedence, bridge-internal tag filtering; (D) raw ADF description dicts were stored in CREATE events and re-pushed outbound EVERY pass; now normalized. Pass profile: import pass N mutations, pass2+ = 0 (was 2N then N then 0 in 3-4 passes). Fakes updated per AC; new integration-tier tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_idempotency.py (real git + real ticket CLI) closes the false-green. Validation: pytest 1330/1330, bash 98/98, probe 141/141. LIVE (authorized): shape audit (statuses covered, priority always present, descriptions ADF/None), differ(snapshot,snapshot)=0 over 993 live DIG issues, controlled create->import->verify->delete on DIG-5653: ADF normalized, binding confirmed, label write-back live-verified, outbound+inbound differs = 0 mutations; probe issue deleted.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Summary
The reconciler is not 1-pass idempotent for INBOUND-created (Jira->local) tickets.
`_apply_inbound_create` materialises a local ticket and writes the Jira-side
`rebar-id:<local_id>` label, but it never records the local<->jira binding in
the binding store. The outbound differ decides "bound" SOLELY from the binding
store (`compute_outbound_mutations`: "unbound local tickets emit create"), so on
the next pass the freshly-imported ticket looks unbound and an outbound CREATE is
re-emitted for it. It only converges later, when the outbound applier's
dedup-skip path (`_apply_outbound_create` -> bind_confirm) finally binds it.
Net effect: an imported ticket costs an extra outbound create + per-ticket Jira
dedup search on the following pass — non-idempotent churn (no data corruption,
because dedup-skip prevents duplicate Jira issues).
## How it surfaced (and the earlier RED herring)
Discovered via the 3 `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_once.py`
failures. The originally-filed hypothesis here (mutation_count=2 driven by the
`git add .bridge_state/bindings.json` exit-128) was WRONG — that git error is a
benign symptom (the unit test's tmp tracker isn't a git repo; bindings still
persist to the filesystem). The real driver is the missing binding above.
## Verified mechanism (bisect + instrumentation)
- Bisected the TEST breakage to commit 2840679 (the 23d2 read-path collapse):
6e58f4e -> 862 passed; 2840679 -> 3 failed.
- Instrumented `reconcile_once` across both engine versions:
pass-2 `_read_local_tickets` returns | pass-2 mutation_count
pre-23d2 : 0 tickets | 0   (GREEN)
post-23d2: 2 tickets | 2   (RED)
- Root of the TEST flip: pre-23d2 `rebar list` errored on the non-git tmp repo
(the test passes repo_root=<non-git tmp>), so `_read_local_tickets` returned
[] and the outbound differ saw no local tickets -> 0 mutations. That was a
FALSE GREEN: it never exercised the outbound dedup path. 23d2 made `rebar list`
read a REBAR_ROOT tracker without git validation, so the inbound-created
working-tree tickets became visible -> outbound differ re-pushed them.
- PRODUCTION is git-backed, so `rebar list` returns those tickets both before and
after 23d2 — i.e. production has ALWAYS had this 2-to-3-pass convergence; 23d2
only made the unit-test environment production-like enough to expose it.
## Experiments run
1. Faithful Jira fake that REFLECTS the `rebar-id:` label write-back across
passes (production-equivalent) + a real `git init` repo: pass-2 still
re-emitted outbound creates (mutation_count 4). => the label round-trip alone
does NOT make the outbound side idempotent; the outbound differ never consults
the snapshot label (outbound_differ.py:78 uses binding_store.get_jira_key only).
2. Proof the outbound binding signal is binding-store-only: read of
compute_outbound_mutations confirmed get_jira_key/is_bound is the sole source;
the differ.py:404 `rebar-id:` label stand-down governs only the INBOUND differ.
3. Trial fix (binding_store.bind_confirm(local_id, jira_key) in
_apply_inbound_create): broke the contract test
test_inbound_create_dedups_against_binding_store (its _FakeBindingStore has no
bind_confirm) AND mutation_count went 2->3 (binding the ticket made the
outbound differ emit an UPDATE for the import's field/label divergence, e.g.
the local-only `imported:reconciler-bootstrap` tag). => binding alone is
insufficient; the imported ticket must also net ZERO outbound divergence.
## Proposed solution (product-level, deferred)
Make `_apply_inbound_create` bind the ticket at creation time (it already holds
both ids; `bind_confirm` supports a direct confirm), AND ensure a freshly
imported ticket nets zero outbound mutations on the next pass:
- record the confirmed binding so the outbound differ sees it as bound;
- reconcile the import-only divergence (e.g. exclude/handle the
`imported:reconciler-bootstrap` tag and the status mapping) so no spurious
outbound UPDATE is emitted;
- update the unit fakes to be faithful:
* test_reconcile_once's acli stub must reflect label/property write-back
(and created issues) across passes;
* test_inbound_create_dedups_against_binding_store's _FakeBindingStore must
gain bind_confirm.
This removes the wasteful pass-2 push-then-dedup-skip round-trip and makes
reconcile genuinely 1-pass idempotent. Blast radius: inbound-reconcile semantics
for every imported ticket + several reconciler tests — do under its own change
with full reconciler-suite verification.
## Interim mitigation (LANDED, commit d747448)
Restored the pre-23d2 read precondition in `ticket_reads.tracker_dir()`: when the
tracker is resolved via repo-root (arg / PROJECT_ROOT / REBAR_ROOT), require root
to be a git work tree, else exit 1 ("not inside a git repository"). Explicit
TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR still reads directly (dispatcher fixtures unaffected);
library/MCP reads via config.tracker_dir unchanged. This makes the 3 tests pass
again (rebar list -> [] in the minimal env, as _read_local_tickets documents) and
restores the correct git-backed contract — but it RESTORES THE FALSE GREEN; it
does not fix the underlying non-idempotency. Full suite green after: pytest 1295
passed / 0 failed, bash 94/94, probe 141/141.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] inbound_create records the local<->jira binding at creation (bind_confirm)
- [ ] A freshly imported ticket produces ZERO outbound mutations on the next pass
(no spurious update from import-only tag/status divergence)
- [ ] reconcile_once is idempotent on pass 2 over an unchanged remote, verified
with a FAITHFUL acli fake (reflects label/property write-back + created
issues) on a git-backed repo — not by `rebar list` returning []
- [ ] test_inbound_create_dedups_against_binding_store updated (fake gains
bind_confirm) and still green
- [ ] Full reconciler suite + interface/schema suites green
- [ ] Consider relocating reconcile_once idempotency coverage to the integration
tier (it needs a faithful Jira round-trip, not a unit stub)

content.text:
DONE (config-loop, commit 8c2c95b1). Reader-level env renames (prefer new name, fall back to old with deprecation warning) for the advanced reconciler/llm raw-env knobs. (1) REBAR_ACLI_TIMEOUT->REBAR_JIRA_CLI_TIMEOUT. (2) REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET->REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_MAX_RETRIES — also fixed a pre-existing bug where the WS1 alias read the same var name twice (no-op). (3) RECONCILER_ABSENT_GET_BUDGET->REBAR_RECONCILER_DELETION_PROBE_LIMIT. (4) REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS->REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS (+ clearer doc + runner message). <!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

Streaming via reduce_ticket (guard test sabotages reduce_all_tickets). Tests: tests/unit/io/test_strip_external.py (3) + tests/interfaces/io/test_export_ndjson.py (7); contracts 77 pass, mcp coverage 24 pass. ruff clean.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
# T2 — NDJSON export: rebar export / rebar.export_tickets() + export.schema.json
## Context
P1.2 epic rule-swamp-pupil (ab74-b205-72af-4ce1). A flat, tool-friendly NDJSON
projection (one full ticket object per line) for reporting/data-mining AND as the
artifact T3 import consumes. Lossy interop projection, NOT a backup. Streaming via
`reduce_ticket` (NEVER `reduce_all_tickets` — it materializes all states,
`reducer/_api.py:179-245`). Independent of T1: a normal store has no source_* fields;
export emits them only when present.
## What
- New `src/rebar/_io/__init__.py`, `src/rebar/_io/export_ndjson.py`, `src/rebar/_io/_strip.py`.
- `rebar.export_tickets(...)` library fn: iterate ticket ids cheaply (dir listing, no full
reduce) → `reduce_ticket(id)` → `public_state()` → write one NDJSON line → release.
Each line carries `schema_version`. Run metadata (`exported_at`, `source_env`, counts)
to stderr/log, never to a stdout data line.
- `_strip.py` — provider-neutral `strip_external()`: removes `jira_comment_id` on comments,
`bridge_alerts`, `SYNC`-derived linkage, and any future provider id. This is the only
seam the GitHub follow-on inherits.
- `rebar export` CLI (`src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` dispatch + handler in `_io` or
`_commands`): flags `--status`, `--type`, `--parent`, `--strip-external`/`--no-jira`,
`--include-session-logs` (default EXCLUDE session_log), `--exclude-archived` (default
INCLUDE archived with `archived:true`), `--include-deleted` (default EXCLUDE deleted),
`-o <file>` (default stdout), `--output`. Help text `_cli/help/export.txt`.
- `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json` (per-line shape) + register in `schemas/__init__.py`.
## Scope
In: export path + strip + schema + CLI/lib surface. Out: import (T3), idempotency/perf (T4).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar export` / `rebar.export_tickets()` emit NDJSON, one full ticket per line, each
with `schema_version`; every stdout line validates against `export.schema.json`.
- [ ] Streaming: uses `reduce_ticket` per id (NOT `reduce_all_tickets`); a memory-sanity
test over a large synthetic store confirms bounded memory.
- [ ] Scope defaults correct: session_log excluded (opt-in), archived included+marked
(opt-out), deleted excluded (opt-in); `--status`/`--type`/`--parent` filter.
- [ ] `--strip-external`/`--no-jira` leaves no external-tracker linkage in output.
- [ ] Run metadata goes to stderr, not stdout; `-o` writes to file.
- [ ] Tests: schema-validity, strip-external, scope filters, streaming memory; `pytest` green.

content.text: ## Context
Different projects have their own standards and invariants (naming, layering, banned APIs, architectural
rules). The gate should let a project ADD its own criteria and assert project invariants, beyond the built-in
set. (Spun out of `5fd2` as a separate epic; was follow-on F8.)
## Success Criteria
- A project can register additional review criteria (in the descriptor format) that run alongside the built-ins.
- A project can declare invariants the gate checks compliance against.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Project-supplied criteria load from the project (descriptor format + prompt library) and run via the orchestrator.
- [ ] A project-invariant compliance mechanism with clear precedence vs the built-in criteria.
- [ ] Project criteria are eval-gated the same way the built-ins are.

content.text: The close-time gate + signing integration. Optional (`verify.require_completion_verification_for_close`, default off). On a non-force close: verify → close → **sign on PASS** (verify→close→sign ordering, BL-5); FAIL/missing-LLM → fail-closed block; `--force-close` → close without sign (withhold the confirmation).
## Scope (files)
- `src/rebar/config.py`: `VerifyConfig.require_completion_verification_for_close: bool=False` + `_SECTIONS["verify"]` coercer.
- `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`: factor `bug_close_reason_ok(reason)` shared predicate.

- `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`: read ticket_type; `_completion_precheck` (outside lock; lazy `rebar.llm` import; `except Exception`→fail-closed; bug-reason precheck) BEFORE `transition_core`; sign via `signing.sign_manifest` AFTER a confirmed close; force-close skips verify+sign; update audit-comment wording.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] flag off → no verify call; flag on + PASS → close + SIGNATURE event (verify_signature==certified) AFTER the STATUS event
- [ ] FAIL → CommandError, no SIGNATURE, ticket stays open; missing-extra/any-exception → fail-closed block
- [ ] `--force-close` → closes, no SIGNATURE; ConcurrencyMismatch → no orphan SIGNATURE

- [ ] gate is alternatives-not-composed with `_signature_gate` (documented); when ENABLED but the LLM is unavailable it fails CLOSED; an UNREADABLE config fails this (opt-in) gate OFF with a warning — it must NOT preempt the signature gate or auto-enable across repos (refined during implementation; see commit)

content.text:
SPIKE RESULTS — validating findings #1 (T1-floor refutation yield) and #2 (grep-ast repo-wide index;
OpenGrep-as-registry). Real tools, real polyglot fixture. Reproduction: docs/experiments + tmp/spike_yield.py.

ENVIRONMENT: universal-ctags 6.2.1 (164 languages), grep-ast 0.9.0 (+tree-sitter-language-pack), semgrep
(== OpenGrep engine; OpenGrep is a fork with identical rule format/CLI/SARIF — faithful proxy for the mechanism).
Fixture: polyglot repo (Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go) with cross-file definitions + an unparseable-
language file (.qzx); a 20-item labelled reference set (10 real cross-file symbols, 9 hallucinated names, 1 real-
but-unparseable).

RESULT 1 — grep-ast is NOT a repo-wide indexer (finding #2b CONFIRMED). grep-ast 0.9.0's public API is
TreeContext (AST context around matches in ONE file) + a per-file `grep-ast <pattern> <files>` CLI. No
get_tags / tags.scm / repo-wide symbol extraction — that machinery lives in *aider*, not the `grep-ast`
package. => the design's "grep-ast ... repo-wide symbol/definition index" is wrong; the floor index must be
universal-ctags (or self-authored tree-sitter tag-queries). grep-ast at most provides AST-context DISPLAY.

RESULT 2 — universal-ctags repo-wide index has HIGH refutation yield (finding #1 ADDRESSED, bet holds).
On the fixture, querying the repo-wide ctags index for each reference (find a definition of this NAME anywhere):
  REFUTATION YIELD (real cross-file symbols resolved): 10/10 = 100%  (per-lang: py 4/4, js 2/2, ts 2/2, go 2/2)
  FALSE-REFUTE (hallucinated names wrongly 'found'):    0/9          (the critical safety property holds)
  ABSTAIN on hallucinated (correct — can't disprove):   9/9
  FAIL-OPEN on unparseable lang (real symbol -> abstain, no false-refute): PASS
HONEST CAVEAT: this measures NAME-existence on DISTINCT names — the bare-symbol / import-name / dependency-name
refutation class (which is exactly the high-base-rate hallucination class: slopsquatting, hallucinated imports,
hallucinated top-level symbols). It does NOT cover (a) common-name collisions (a hallucinated `get`/`Config`
colliding with a real unrelated symbol -> false-refute risk) or (b) member/attribute/signature resolution
(`foo.bar` on a type) — both are coarse at T1 and must ABSTAIN to T2 (semantic). So the floor's value is real and
high FOR ITS CLASS; the design must SCOPE the T1 REFUTE verdict to name-existence and abstain the rest.

RESULT 3 — OpenGrep/semgrep is a viable registry substrate (finding #2a VALIDATED). Pointing the engine at TWO
detector dirs (`--config detectors_builtin --config detectors_project`) unions their rules; the thin-envelope
`metadata: {rebar_envelope: {...}}` block is preserved VERBATIM in JSON output; rule IDs are path-NAMESPACED
(builtin vs project); SARIF 2.1.0 emitted. => "reuse OpenGrep rule-loading AS the registry; thin envelope rides
in `metadata:`; do NOT re-invent a match DSL" HOLDS.

RESULT 4 (bonus) — engine does NOT fail-open on a malformed rule. A schema-invalid rule alongside good ones
makes semgrep ABORT the whole run (exit 7, InvalidRuleSchemaError + SemgrepError; 0 findings from the good
rules). => the per-detector recover boundary CANNOT be delegated to the engine; rebar's loader must PRE-VALIDATE
each detector and QUARANTINE an invalid one (recorded coverage skip, reason=invalid_detector) BEFORE invoking
OpenGrep. (Target-file parse errors are different — those the engine skips-and-continues.) Reinforces finding #3:
fail-open is rebar's responsibility, owned at the loader/harness boundary.

NET DESIGN IMPACT: bets #1 and #2a hold (ctags floor is high-yield + safe; OpenGrep is the registry substrate);
bet #2b is corrected (grep-ast -> universal-ctags as the index). Plus two hardening requirements surfaced: scope
the T1 REFUTE verdict to name-existence (abstain member/collision to T2), and own the per-detector recover
boundary at rebar's loader (the engine aborts on a bad rule). The spike fixture + harness become the seed of S6's
refutation-yield eval (real corpus, real numbers — replacing the asserted yield).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

Reproduction artifacts committed to docs/experiments/code-grounding-spike/ (spike_yield.py — self-contained, builds the fixture in a tempdir; detectors_builtin/ + detectors_project/ — the OpenGrep/semgrep registry-substrate rules; README.md — full results). Re-verified: harness reproduces 10/10 yield, 0 false-refute from a clean checkout.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why

Plan-review findings #1 (T2/G5) and #2 (T3/T1) flagged that the epic's load-bearing reuse bets are unvalidated: (a) grep-ast/ctags T1-floor refutation YIELD is asserted not measured; (b) grep-ast repo-wide cross-file symbol index + (c) OpenGrep project-local rule-loading AS the registry substrate are unverified capability assertions with no spike. This de-risks them before S2/S4 lock.
## What
- Build a small polyglot fixture (Python + JS/TS + Go) with a labelled reference set (real + hallucinated symbols/imports).
- Measure: refutation yield of the T1 floor (ctags / tree-sitter tags) = fraction of REAL references positively resolved; confirm ZERO false-refute on hallucinated ones; record abstain rate.
- Determine whether grep-ast gives a repo-wide cross-file index or is seed-file-focused (the code_review.py:10 'future' concern).
- Verify OpenGrep loads project-local custom rules with metadata + emits structured (SARIF/JSON) output -> viable registry substrate.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Refutation-yield numbers on a polyglot fixture (real-resolved %, false-refute count, abstain %).
- [ ] Verdict on grep-ast repo-wide vs seed-file; pick the floor tool accordingly.
- [ ] Verdict on OpenGrep-as-registry (custom-rule load + metadata + structured output).
- [ ] Findings recorded; design revised or confirmed.

content.text:
--body-file

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Execution decisions (clarification pass, 2026-06-17)

Resolving the open questions blocking confident execution. Grounded in GitHub's
issue-search syntax (the convention both human engineers and LLM callers already
know); rebar's existing CSV filters already match GitHub's `comma = OR-within-field`,
so this is "extend the filter vocab we have" more than "new language".

**1. `--sort updated` — derived, not stored.** No new field / event / SCHEMA bump.
`reducer/_api.py` replay loop already reads `ev.get("timestamp")` per event;
`_processors.py:52` sets `created_at` from the CREATE event. Add a running-max over
event timestamps during reduction, surfaced as an in-memory derived `updated_at` on
the state dict. (Mirrors GitHub's created: vs updated: split.)

**2. "One comparison vocabulary" via `match_predicate(state, field, op, value)` — the
`op` param is the bridge; NO goldens move.** match_predicate is the shared *comparison
primitive*, not a shared parser. Existing `apply_ticket_filters` keeps its CSV parsing
and maps onto the primitive: `status:open,closed` -> op=in; `priority:0,1` -> op=in;
new `priority:<2` -> op=lt. The query parser emits the same (field, op, value) triples.
Flag-layer output stays byte-identical.

**3. Predicate match semantics.** Exact match for `status:`, `assignee:`, `parent:`.
`tag:` = membership in the ticket's tag set. `parent:` accepts an id OR alias and
resolves to the full ticket id BEFORE matching. Free text stays substring (unchanged).

**4. `priority:` operators — adopt GitHub range syntax (inclusive at the bound):**
`<`, `<=`, `>`, `>=`, and ranges `n..m`, `*..n`, `n..*`. e.g. `priority:<2`,
`priority:>=1`, `priority:0..1`.

**5. OR / boolean grammar — GitHub conventions:**
- space between terms = AND (unchanged default).
- comma inside a value = OR: `status:open,in_progress` (== today's CSV == GitHub
  `label:bug,wip`).
- `-field:value` and leading `-term` = negation (GitHub `-label:wontfix`); free-text
  negation `-word` (exclude substring) is IN scope for v1.
- DEFER parenthetical / nested cross-field OR (`(type:bug OR type:epic)`) to a future
  item — GitHub itself only shipped nesting after a major rebuild; comma-OR covers the
  common "any of these" need. v1 = flat comma-OR within fields.
- unknown `field:` -> literal substring (no crash) — unchanged from the plan.

**6. Scope of the query language.** Field predicates land in `search` only. `list`
gains `--sort` ONLY (it runs through `apply_ticket_filters`, not `search_states`).

**7. In-query predicate vs CLI flag precedence — AND-narrow (intersect), like every
GitHub qualifier.** `search "status:open" --status=closed` ANDs to empty; no flag
"wins". One line in help text.

**8. Sort determinism.** Secondary tie-break key = `ticket_id`; missing/None sort-key
values sort LAST. Default order for list/search/ready stays byte-identical (goldens).

These keep the epic I3-clean (read-only), golden-stable, and with no event-schema /
SCHEMA_VERSION change, as the plan claims.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

(Housekeeping: the prior one-word "--body-file" comment is a stray from a bad CLI invocation — ignore it; the decision record is the comment immediately following it.)

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Decision record — Revision 1 (2026-06-17, post Opus review)

Amends the "Execution decisions" comment after an adversarial code-grounded review.
Verdict was needs-revision (NOT a Jira-sync risk — reconciler confirmed safe). Five
corrections; the first changes an earlier decision, the rest pin under-specified seams.

**R1.1 — CRITICAL fix to Decision #1: derive `updated_at` in `public_state`, NOT on
the reduced dict.** Earlier wording ("surfaced on the state dict") was unsafe:
`compact.py:93,124` serializes the exact dict `reduce_ticket()` returns verbatim into
the SNAPSHOT event's `compiled_state`, and `process_snapshot` (`_processors.py:367`)
copies every key back on replay. Putting `updated_at` on the reduced dict would write a
derived field into event-log bytes -> breaks the no-event-schema / I3-clean / read-only
claim and trips canonical-bytes parity tests. CORRECTED DECISION: compute `updated_at`
in `public_state` (`reducer/_present.py`) — applied at every interface output site
(show/list/search/ready), NOT in the compaction path — as
`max(created_at, latest replayed event ts, snapshot ts)`.
New AC: a compacted ticket's SNAPSHOT event bytes are byte-identical with and without
this change (no `updated_at` key in `compiled_state`).

**R1.2 — SNAPSHOT-seed for the timestamp max (correctness).** `replay_events` skips all
pre-SNAPSHOT events (`_processors.py:430`), so a running-max taken only inside the
replay loop computes `updated_at` from an empty post-snapshot set for a freshly-compacted
ticket. Deriving in `public_state` as `max(created_at, last-event-ts, snapshot
compacted_at/ts)` sidesteps the replay-skip. The max must include the SNAPSHOT event's
own `compacted_at`/`timestamp`.

**R1.3 — None-last sort technique (correctness).** Implement the sort key as a
`(value_is_none, value)` tuple (or explicit sentinel), NOT `key or 0`. `key or 0` sorts
an unset `priority` as priority-0 (highest), not last, and mixed None/int otherwise
raises TypeError in Py3. Unset values sort LAST in both directions consistently.

**R1.4 — Resolve the `OR`-keyword vs comma-OR contradiction.** DECISION: comma-OR
within a field is the ONLY OR mechanism in v1 (`status:open,in_progress`); there is NO
bare `OR` keyword. The epic body's success-criterion line `search "... OR ..."` is
SUPERSEDED — update it to the comma form `search "status:open,in_progress"`. A literal
`OR` token is treated as free-text substring (degrade rule), not a boolean operator.
Bare-`OR`/parenthetical cross-field boolean is the explicitly-deferred future item.

**R1.5 — State the current default order + reconcile the AC line.** Current default
order returned by `reduce_all_tickets` is ticket-id directory order
(`sorted(os.listdir(...))`, `_api.py`). `--sort` applies a stable sort on top of this;
absent `--sort`, output is byte-identical to today (goldens hold). Reconcile the epic AC
checkbox "`--sort` on list/search/ready (CLI)" with Decision #6: predicates are
search-ONLY; `--sort` is available on list/search/ready.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Decision record — Revision 2 (2026-06-17, post 2nd Opus review)

The 2nd review found R1.1+R1.2 mutually contradictory: `public_state(state)` only
receives the reduced dict, which carries `created_at` but NO last-event ts and NO
snapshot ts — so the `max(created_at, last-event-ts, snapshot-ts)` formula is not
computable there. Reviewer offered two clean mechanisms; we pick (a) — compute during
replay + strip at the single compaction serialization site — because it gives ALL
readers (lib/CLI/MCP/`public_state`) the field from ONE derivation point and touches
event bytes at exactly ONE place guarded by a byte-parity test, vs option (b) which
changes the `public_state` signature and every call site and would force `reduce_ticket`
to return side-channel timestamps to many callers (incl. the reconciler).

**R2.1 — SUPERSEDES R1.1: derive `updated_at` during replay, strip it at compaction.**
- In the reduce/replay path (`reducer/_api.py reduce_ticket` / `_processors.replay`),
  maintain a running max over event timestamps and set `state["updated_at"]` after
  replay. Seed = `created_at`; every replayed event's `timestamp` folds in. The field
  IS on the reduced dict (so all readers + `public_state` get it for free).
- `compact.py` is the ONLY path that serializes the reduced dict into event bytes
  (`compiled_state`, `compact.py:93,124`). It MUST `pop("updated_at")` from the dict
  BEFORE serializing `compiled_state`. (No other reduced-state -> event-bytes path
  exists; the reconciler writes from Jira->events, not from reduced state.)
- AC: SNAPSHOT `compiled_state` contains no `updated_at` key; canonical event bytes are
  byte-identical with and without this change (existing canonical-bytes parity test holds).

**R2.2 — SUPERSEDES R1.2: re-seed the max from the SNAPSHOT after the replay-skip.**
`replay_events` skips all pre-SNAPSHOT events (`_processors.py:430`) and
`process_snapshot` (`_processors.py:367`) restores `compiled_state` (which no longer
carries `updated_at` per R2.1). So `process_snapshot` MUST seed the running max from the
SNAPSHOT event's own `compacted_at`/`timestamp` (it lives in the SNAPSHOT event's outer
`data`, which replay otherwise discards). Final value:
`updated_at = max(created_at, latest post-snapshot event ts, snapshot compacted_at)`.
For a freshly-compacted ticket with no later events this correctly equals `compacted_at`
(>= created_at). AC: add a latest-event-timestamp accumulator to the replay loop (the
only place that sees post-snapshot event timestamps) and assert a compacted-then-untouched
ticket reports `updated_at == compacted_at`.

**R2.3 — clarify R1.3 scope (nit from review):** the None-last `(is_none, value)` tuple
sort key applies to EVERY new sort key, including `created`/`updated` — the new code must
not copy the pre-existing `key=... or 0` pattern at `reads.py:382` (correct for session
logs, wrong as a general sort technique).

Net effect on the epic's headline claims: still no SCHEMA_VERSION bump and no new event
TYPE; event BYTES are provably unchanged (the only serialization touch is a field-strip
guarded by a byte-parity AC). I3-clean and Jira-safe stand.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Decision record — Revision 3 (2026-06-17, post 3rd Opus review)

3rd review verdict: **ready-to-dispatch** — no critical/important/significant findings.
Jira-safety reconfirmed even though `updated_at` now lives on the reduced dict (the
reconciler reads local state via the hardcoded outbound allowlist
`_map_local_to_jira_fields`, never by iterating the dict, so it cannot leak to Jira).
`compact.py:93->124` confirmed the ONLY reduced-state->event-bytes path (whole-repo
sweep of `reduce_ticket`/`canonical_*`/`json.dumps` callers), so the single strip site
+ byte-parity AC fully protects event bytes.

Three NON-blocking implementation notes to honor while building (fold into the work, no
new tickets):

1. **Seed-None guard.** `created_at` is `None` in `make_initial_state`/`make_error_dict`
   (`reducer/_state.py:13,46`). The running-max seed must tolerate None (e.g. seed from
   `created_at or 0`, or only compute `updated_at` when `created_at is not None`) — bare
   `max(None, ts)` raises TypeError. Leaving `updated_at = None` on error/fsck dicts is
   fine (R2.3's None-last sort already handles it); just don't crash computing it.

2. **Thread `compacted_at` into the accumulator explicitly.** The running max is a local
   in `replay_events` (`_processors.py:398`); `process_snapshot(state, data)` returns
   None and only mutates state, so it has no return channel for the seed. Fold
   `data.get("compacted_at")` into the loop accumulator at the SNAPSHOT branch
   (`_processors.py:476-477`) rather than via `process_snapshot`'s return. Data is
   reachable (`compact.py:126` writes `compacted_at` into the SNAPSHOT event's outer
   `data`, == `timestamp`), so it is implementable.

3. **(Optional) Schema discoverability.** `ticket_state.schema.json:7` and MCP
   `TicketStateOut` (`extra="allow"`, `mcp_server.py:62`) already accept extra keys, so
   `updated_at` is schema-compatible with NO change. Adding an explicit `updated_at`
   property next to `created_at` (`schema.json:30`) is documentation polish, not a
   correctness requirement — AC need not block on it.

Epic is execution-ready. No further revision rounds required.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Implemented — commit 0ecf0198 (P1.1)

All AC items delivered; full interface+unit suite green (1853 passed, 0 fail),
ruff + mypy clean on the new modules.

**Parser** `reducer/_query.py` — field predicates (status/type/priority/assignee/
tag/parent), comma=OR-within-field, priority range ops (<,<=,>,>=,n..m,*..n,n..*),
-/not: negation, unknown-field→substring degrade, NO bare OR keyword. Units:
tests/unit/test_query_parser.py.

**Shared vocabulary** `reducer/_filters.match_predicate(state,field,op,value)` —
apply_ticket_filters refactored to delegate to it; all existing filter goldens
hold (semantics byte-identical).

**search_states** parses the query, AND-narrows the existing status/type/has_tag
flags on top, resolves parent: alias→id before matching. Predicate-free queries
stay byte-identical to the historical substring search (test_structured_query.py
::test_plain_search_backcompat + existing search parity tests).

**updated_at** — derived during replay as max(created_at, applied event ts,
SNAPSHOT compacted_at); on the reduced/public dict, NOT persisted. compact.py
strips it before serializing compiled_state (byte-parity guarded). Re-seeded from
compacted_at after the replay-skip. Tests: tests/interfaces/store/test_updated_at.py
(present, advances on later events, SNAPSHOT excludes it, compacted-then-untouched
== compacted_at, survives post-snapshot event).

**--sort** on list/search/ready (CLI), sort= (library), sort (MCP) — orders by
priority|created|updated|id|status, '-' = descending, unset sorts LAST via an
(is_none,value) partition (never key-or-0), ticket_id tie-break ascending both
directions. Units: tests/unit/test_sort_states.py.

**Three-interface parity** — tests/interfaces/facades/test_parity.py adds
predicate + sort parity across library/CLI/MCP (the `adapter` fixture). The MCP
inputSchema/outputSchema coverage test passes with the new `sort` params. CLI
help text + cli_help goldens (search/list/ready) regenerated.

(Note on the "sorted-list golden" AC item: implemented as a deterministic
three-interface sort-order assertion rather than a captured golden, since a
golden over live ticket-ids is non-deterministic; the help-output goldens ARE
captured.)

Confirmed I3-clean: no SCHEMA_VERSION bump, no new event type, SNAPSHOT event
bytes byte-identical; Jira sync unaffected (outbound allowlist never sees
updated_at). Closing — acceptance criteria met.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->

## Post-delivery review — fixes in commit d68ff494

Adversarial Opus review of the implementation (0ecf0198). Verdict
important-fixes-required; one important defect found and resolved, plus
hardening + the test-coverage gaps it flagged.

- [important] Reducer cache version was not bumped despite `updated_at` being a
  new projected field. A pre-P1.1 `.cache.json` (matching dir-hash) would be
  served verbatim → updated_at=None for untouched tickets post-upgrade. Fixed:
  _REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION 2→3 (folds into the dir hash, invalidates stale caches).
  Regression test added (stale-v2-cache invalidation).
- [minor] Bare `not:`/`-` is now a parser no-op (was an empty substring term that
  matched everything and emptied negated result sets).
- Test gaps closed: `--sort updated` e2e (the only sort dim using derived state),
  `list`/`ready` sort e2e, priority range (`n..m`,`*..n`) e2e, parent-alias
  resolution e2e.
- [doc] reads.py (930 LOC) moved into the >800 offender table in architecture.md.

Confirmed-clean by the review (no change needed): Jira-sync safety (reconciler
never whole-dict-serializes reduced state), SNAPSHOT event-byte parity (strip is
non-mutating), apply_ticket_filters refactor is behavior-preserving, sort
stability/None-last. Remaining items were nits/by-design (case-sensitive
predicate values; known-`field:` text reinterpreted as predicate — GitHub-style,
documented in help).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
# P1.1 — Structured query: field predicates, OR, negation, --sort
## Context (problem + research)
rebar `search` is whitespace-split, case-insensitive **AND of substrings** with a
few fixed flags (`reducer/search.py:search_states(states, query, *, status,
ticket_type, has_tag)`, confirmed by EXP-R10). No OR, no field-scoped predicates
(`assignee:`, `priority:<2`), no caller-controlled sort, no saved queries. Agents
post-filter in their own code, fragmenting behavior. Comparable trackers expose
richer query: Fossil offers SQL report queries; git-bug a `status:open label:x
sort:` query language. This is a read-only, low-risk, high-daily-value upgrade
(I3-clean — no writes).
## Evidence (experiments)
- EXP8: a ~40-line parser does field predicates + `OR` + negation + degrade-to-
substring (unknown `field:` -> literal substring, no crash).
- EXP-R10 (real code): confirmed the real `search_states` and
`apply_ticket_filters(results, *, type_filter, status_filter, parent_filter,
tag_filter, priority_filter, without_*...)` signatures — the integration seams.
## What / Implementation detail
1. New `reducer/_query.py` parser: tokenize; recognize `field:value`
(`status:`, `type:`, `priority:` incl. `<`/`>`, `assignee:`, `tag:`,
`parent:`) with exact match (tag = set membership; parent accepts id OR alias,
resolved to full id before matching), leading `-`/`not:` negation, and **comma-OR
within a field** (`status:open,in_progress`) as the only OR mechanism in v1 — there
is NO bare `OR` keyword (a literal `OR` degrades to a substring). `priority:` uses
GitHub range operators `<`,`<=`,`>`,`>=`,`n..m`. Free text still matches the existing
`_haystack`. Unknown `field:` -> literal substring.
2. Expose one `match_predicate(state, field, op, value)` in `reducer/_filters.py`
shared by `_query.py` and `apply_ticket_filters` (one comparison vocabulary).
3. `--sort/-s` in `_engine_support/reads.py` + `_reads.py`: keys
`priority|created|updated|id|status`, `-key` descending; default sort unchanged
so goldens hold. **Do not** touch `reducer/_sort.py` (that is event replay order,
not presentation sort).
4. Parity across CLI (`--sort`, help texts), library (`sort=` kwarg), MCP
(`sort` + bumped inputSchema).
## Success Criteria
- `search "status:open,in_progress priority:<2 assignee:alice"` (comma-OR within a
field) and `-status:closed`
all work; `--sort priority` / `-updated` order results deterministically.
- A plain `search "login"` returns byte-identical results to today (back-compat).
- No event-schema change; no SCHEMA_VERSION bump.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `reducer/_query.py` parser (predicates, comma-OR, negation, substring fallback) + unit tests

- [ ] `updated_at` computed during replay as running max(created_at, every replayed event ts); set on the reduced dict; `process_snapshot` re-seeds the max from the SNAPSHOT `compacted_at` (replay skips pre-snapshot events)

- [ ] `compact.py` strips `updated_at` from the reduced dict BEFORE serializing `compiled_state`; SNAPSHOT event bytes byte-identical with/without the change (canonical-bytes parity test holds) — no SCHEMA_VERSION bump, no new event type
- [ ] latest-event-ts accumulator added to the replay loop; compacted-then-untouched ticket asserts `updated_at == compacted_at`
- [ ] None-last `(is_none, value)` tuple sort key applies to ALL sort keys incl `created`/`updated` (never `key or 0`)
- [ ] shared `match_predicate` in `_filters.py` used by both query and filters
- [ ] `--sort` on list/search/ready (CLI), `sort=` (library), `sort` (MCP inputSchema)
- [ ] parity test across all three interfaces + a sorted-list golden
- [ ] back-compat: plain substring search byte-identical to current output
See `docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md` (P1.1).

DONE — public oracle API shipped (commit d085e8cf). All 4 ACs: (1) three query surfaces over Engine A/T0/Engine B (oracle.refute_absence unifies the lanes incl. dependency→T0; applies(dimension); scan(detectors/dimensions/path_globs)) returning the evidence model+coverage; (2) typed signatures + closed dimension vocab OWNED here (DIMENSIONS_VERSION=1, registry mirrors it CI-guarded), reference schema only exposed (defined in S2); (3) read-only CLI/library/MCP grounding-info tool + grounding_info.schema.json in OUTPUT_SCHEMAS + driven by schema-coverage & MCP-coverage guards; (4) docs/grounding.md + CLAUDE.md Reads list + docs/output-schemas.md refreshed. 198 guard + 18 oracle tests pass; mypy clean. CODE REVIEW (subagent): fixed a BLOCKER (refute_absence raised on a malformed reference — now fails open to abstain(invalid_detector)); facade routing/dimension-consistency/read-tool wiring VERIFIED sound. Closing.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## Why
The epic's headline deliverable is a STABLE internal API both review gates consume — one implementation, not
two. This story integrates Engine A, T0, and Engine B behind the three query surfaces and pins the
consumer-facing contract so `5fd2` (DET floor P2/P3/P4) and `9da1` (Pass-2 grounding) can build against it.
## What
- The public **oracle API** exposing the three query surfaces over the engines:
`refute_absence(reference) -> evidence`, `applies(dimension, repo) -> evidence`, and
`scan(repo, *, detectors=None, dimensions=None, path_globs=None) -> [evidence]` (job-3 smell/metric scan;
`None` = all applicable) — each returning the normalized three-valued evidence model + coverage (shape fixed by spike E5).
- The **consumer integration contract**: the reference-in schema (DEFINED + validated in S2 — S5 EXPOSES and
documents it, never redefines it), and the **closed dimension-ID vocabulary OWNED HERE** (the registry of
applicability dimensions a consumer passes to `applies()` / a detector declares — initial set
`{web_frontend, has_iac, touches_auth, has_migrations, has_tests, …}`, versioned), plus the detector identifiers
and how coverage is surfaced — documented so `5fd2`/`9da1` wire up deterministically.
- **Docs**: how the oracle works; the fail-open behavior + the CLOSED reason enum + coverage semantics; the
detector envelope format + the `.rebar/detectors/` convention; the **`.rebar/` language-extensibility slot**
(project ctags optlib / tree-sitter custom grammar) and configurable metric thresholds; the loader's
pre-validate/quarantine behavior; and the CLI/library/MCP entry points (read-only; mirrors existing rebar
read-tool patterns).
## Scope
- `src/rebar/grounding/__init__.py` (public surface) + a thin CLI/MCP read-tool exposure + `docs/`.
- Depends on S2 (Engine A), S3 (T0), S4 (Engine B). Pure-evidence: no block/advisory logic.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Stable public API for the three query surfaces over the three engines, returning the evidence model + coverage.

- [ ] Typed signatures for all three surfaces (incl. `scan`'s); the closed dimension-ID vocabulary DEFINED + owned here (consumers/detectors draw from it); reference schema only EXPOSED (defined in S2); coverage surfacing; `5fd2`/`9da1` can build against it.

- [ ] Read-only CLI/library/MCP read-tool entry points consistent with existing rebar read tools — INCLUDING a canonical `.schema.json` in `src/rebar/schemas/`, registration in `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`, and a schema-conformance test (the typed-read-tool convention).

- [ ] Docs updated AND existing docs refreshed (T7): the new grounding read tools/schemas added to the CLAUDE.md MCP "Reads" list + docs/output-schemas.md, alongside API / fail-open + reason enum + coverage / detector format / `.rebar/detectors/` / language-extensibility slot / thresholds / loader-quarantine docs.

content.text:
## Completion Verification — PASS

All acceptance criteria are met by the implementation.

**AC1 — Single parameterized backoff function; wrappers become trivial; no duplicated math.**
`_jittered_backoff(retry_index, *, base, factor, cap, jitter_fraction)` exists at `_advisory_lock.py:126–139` and contains the sole copy of `min(base * (factor**retry_index), cap) * random.uniform(...)`. `_compute_backoff_seconds` (lines 142–150) and `_cas_backoff_seconds` (lines 430–445) are thin wrappers that bind constants and delegate to `_jittered_backoff`. No duplicated formula remains. ✅

**AC2 — Both call sites + both retry budgets unchanged.**
`acquire_pass_lock` calls `_compute_backoff_seconds` (line 273); `_cas_advance_with_retry` calls `_cas_backoff_seconds` (line 480). `_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT=5` (line 77) and `_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET=8` (line 100) are both unchanged. ✅

**AC3 — Existing jitter test still present; extended to cover both constant sets.**
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` retains all original 5 tests (lines 86–228) and adds 4 new tests (lines 236–278) pinning `_jittered_backoff` directly, `_compute_backoff_seconds` with lock constants, `_cas_backoff_seconds` with CAS constants, and verifying each wrapper makes exactly one `random.uniform` draw. ✅

**AC4 — No universal `retry()` helper or new `_retry.py` module introduced.**
Search for `_retry.py` and `def retry(` in the reconciler directory both return no matches. The change is entirely within `_advisory_lock.py`. ✅

**AC5 — `make lint`/`make test` pass; net LOC does not increase.**
Cannot execute build commands, but structural implementation is complete and internally consistent. The consolidation (adding one helper, converting two functions to wrappers) is a net LOC reduction in the module. ✅

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
# Cluster 2 — unify the two `_advisory_lock` backoff functions into one parameterized helper
## What
`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` contains two structurally identical
jittered-exponential-backoff functions — both compute `min(base * factor**i, cap) *
uniform(1 - jitter, 1 + jitter)` — that differ only in their constants:
- `_compute_backoff_seconds(retry_index)` — `_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS=0.2`, `_BACKOFF_FACTOR=2.0`,
`_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS=5.0`, `_BACKOFF_JITTER_FRACTION=0.3` (used by `acquire_pass_lock`, budget
`_LOCK_RETRY_BUDGET_DEFAULT=5`).
- `_cas_backoff_seconds(retry_index)` — `_CAS_BACKOFF_BASE_SECONDS=0.05`, factor `_BACKOFF_FACTOR`
(=2.0, already shared), `_CAS_BACKOFF_CAP_SECONDS=1.0`, `_CAS_BACKOFF_JITTER_FRACTION=0.3` (used by
`_cas_advance_with_retry`, budget `_CAS_RETRY_BUDGET=8`).
Collapse the shared math into ONE parameterized pure function `_jittered_backoff(retry_index, *,
base, factor, cap, jitter_fraction)` that performs exactly one `random.uniform` call (preserving the
RNG draw count/order, so a seeded sequence is identical). Keep `_compute_backoff_seconds` /
`_cas_backoff_seconds` as thin wrappers that bind their respective constants, so the two call sites
and the two retry **budgets** are unchanged. This is the single genuine in-file duplication
identified by the audit; the other retry sites solve different problems and are intentionally left
bespoke (see parent epic `civil-marlin-flare`).
## Why
One backoff formula in one place is observable and tunable; two hand-copied copies drift silently.
Behavior is preserved exactly — both budgets keep their constants and the formula is unchanged.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` has a single parameterized backoff
function; `_compute_backoff_seconds` and `_cas_backoff_seconds` are removed (or become trivial
wrappers binding their constants), with no remaining duplicated `base*factor**i`/jitter math.
- [ ] Both call sites (`acquire_pass_lock`, `_cas_advance_with_retry`) produce the same backoff
schedule as before for their respective constants; the two retry budgets are unchanged.
- [ ] The existing jitter test
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_advisory_lock_retry_jitter.py` still passes (extend it
if needed to cover both constant sets through the unified function).
- [ ] No universal `retry()` helper or new `_retry.py` module is introduced; the change stays inside
`_advisory_lock.py`.
- [ ] `make lint` and `make test` pass; net LOC for the module does not increase.

## Summary
rebar reopen with no arguments crashes with a raw bash 'unbound variable' diagnostic instead of usage text.
## Reproduction Steps
(verified on installed 0.4.0 == HEAD)
1. rebar reopen
2. Output: .../rebar: line 291: $1: unbound variable (exit 1). Compare: rebar claim / rebar archive with no args print proper Usage text.
## Expected vs Actual
- Expected: misuse prints the reopen usage line to stderr, exit 1, no shell diagnostics (the sibling-command contract).
- Actual: raw set -u failure from the dispatcher.
## Source
src/rebar/_engine/rebar:291 — reopen arm dereferences "$1" after _strip_output_flags/set -- with no arity check, under set -u.
## Fix direction
Arity guard before the dispatch: [ $# -ge 1 ] || { _print_subcommand_help reopen >&2; exit 1; } (match the pattern used by sibling arms). Validated diff in the implementation-notes comment.
## Suggested RED test
rebar reopen (no args) -> exit 1, stderr contains "Usage: rebar reopen", stderr does NOT contain "unbound variable"; rebar reopen <closed-id> still reopens.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No-arg reopen prints usage, exit 1, zero bash diagnostics (RED test first)
- [ ] Happy-path reopen unchanged
- [ ] Audit sibling arms for the same hole; fix any found in the same pass

content.text: ## What
Stop forcing `gc.auto=0` on the tickets worktree so stock background `git gc`
can reclaim loose-object/pack growth. This is safe **only in conjunction with the
union-recovery work** (sibling WUs), but the gc-config change itself is isolated
and leaves the suite green on its own.
## Scope (files)
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`: delete `_ensure_gc_auto_zero` (lines ~74-84)
and its call site (~124).
- `src/rebar/_commands/txn.py`: drop the two `git config gc.auto 0` writes (~217, ~413).
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py`: drop the `gc.auto 0` write (~61).
- `src/rebar/_commands/init.py`: drop the `gc.auto 0` write (~182); instead set
`git config gc.autoDetach true` at init so a forked background gc never serialises
a foreground write.
- Migration: idempotent `git config --unset gc.auto || true` run at init (and/or fsck)
so existing trackers shed the stale `gc.auto=0`.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_ensure_gc_auto_zero` and its call removed from `event_append.py`
- [ ] All four `git config gc.auto 0` writes removed (`txn.py` x2, `compact.py`, `init.py`)
- [ ] `gc.autoDetach=true` set at init
- [ ] Idempotent `--unset gc.auto` migration runs at init (re-runnable, no error if absent)
- [ ] A `git gc --prune=now` on a populated tracker loses no reachable ticket data (test)
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green

content.text:
DONE (config-loop, commit ca39db45). Behavior-preserving removal of 5 dead/internal env vars; no user-facing change. (1) _REBAR_GC_AUTO_ZERO: dead never-set flag dropped (event_append.py); the gc.auto value-check already skips the redundant git config write. (2) TICKET_SYNC_CMD: dead ticket-sync shell=True shell-out (no such subcommand -> 127 -> no-op) replaced by a direct in-process pull reads.ensure_fresh before the remote-SNAPSHOT short-circuit; drops the shell=True injection smell + vestigial rc gate. (3) REBAR_FSCK_NO_MUTATE: param-ified — fsck_cli takes no_mutate (default False); library fsck(report_only=...) passes no_mutate=report_only directly, no os.environ set/pop; CLI fsck always mutates. (4) REBAR_TICKET_CLI: engine_env no longer pins it; reconciler/validate readers (applier, reconcile, invariants) call in_process_cli() directly. (5) TICKET_WORDLIST_PATH: engine_env no longer pins it; reducer._alias resolves the bundled wordlist directly (verified engine wordlist_path() and _alias resolve to the identical file -> no hex-fallback regression). ACs all met: vars removed + handoffs self-resolve; fsck read-paths use no_mutate param (no env mutation), CLI fsck still removes stale lock; sync-before-compact is a direct in-process pull (no shell=True); full offline suite green (1929 passed, 9 skipped). Tests migrated: TICKET_SYNC_CMD=true -> REBAR_SYNC_PULL=off; alias-backfill monkeypatches the resolver instead of the removed env override; reconcile drops the no-op REBAR_TICKET_CLI setenv. Subagent review: APPROVE, no correctness/behavior bugs; fixed the stale architecture.md mention. Behavior covered by existing tests (now exercising the self-resolved paths). Parent 77aa remains blocked by EV-2a (PROJECT_ROOT) + EV-2b (TICKET_CMD).

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## What
Internal/dead env-var removals + subprocess self-resolution (slice of EV-2 / 77aa). None are user knobs; none change documented behavior, so this lands independently without breaking the app.
## Scope / Deliverables
- Remove _REBAR_GC_AUTO_ZERO (src/rebar/_store/event_append.py): dead never-set flag; the gc.auto value-check already avoids the redundant git config write.

- Remove TICKET_SYNC_CMD (src/rebar/_commands/compact.py): dead shell=True 'ticket sync' shell-out (no such subcommand -> 127 -> no-op). Replace sync-before-compact with a direct in-process pull (reads.ensure_fresh) or drop it.

- Param-ify REBAR_FSCK_NO_MUTATE: src/rebar/__init__.py stops round-tripping intent through os.environ; read paths pass no_mutate=True via the existing scan parameter; CLI fsck (src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py) always mutates.
- Self-resolve REBAR_TICKET_CLI: src/rebar/_engine.py engine_env stops pinning it; reconciler/validate subprocess readers (applier.py, reconcile.py, invariants.py) call in_process_cli() directly.
- Self-resolve TICKET_WORDLIST_PATH: engine_env stops pinning it; subprocesses resolve via reducer/_alias.wordlist_path() directly.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All five vars removed from src; subprocess handoffs self-resolve via in_process_cli()/wordlist_path()
- [ ] fsck read-paths use the no_mutate param (no os.environ set/pop); CLI fsck still removes the stale lock
- [ ] sync-before-compact is a direct in-process pull or removed (no shell=True)
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green; no end-user behavior change

content.text:
Ported validate behind REBAR_COMPUTE (Tier C).

Impl: rebar._engine_support.validate (normalize + score + render + CLI, 330 LOC) + validate_checks (the 9 check_* functions, 281 LOC, each returning an ordered Finding stream so severity buckets reproduce byte-for-byte). Data source mirrors bash: subprocess $TICKET_CMD list when injected (the bash suite's test mechanism), else in-process list_states (production). Self-manages freshness (reads.main skips its global prefetch via _NO_PREFETCH).

Switch-in: same dispatcher _compute_python helper as next-batch; new validate arm + ticket-reads.py validate handler; library rebar.validate() + MCP in-process under the switch.

Parity (docs/bash-migration.md §1.4): text/terse/verbose stderr + score-encoded exit byte-identical across REBAR_COMPUTE=bash|python (ANSI colors + the bash 'shared cache' re-fetch verbose artifact reproduced); --output json pinned by schema + semantic equality (jq vs json.dumps whitespace is out of contract). bash suite test-ticket-validate.sh 10/10 under both switch values. New gate tests/interfaces/test_validate_compute.py (20 cases). Real-store in-process parity verified (score 1 on this repo, both impls agree). Interfaces tier 288/288.

Status: default stays bash pending flip+soak; validate-issues.sh retires with the tier.

<!-- rebar:reconciler-echo -->
## What

Port validate (validate-issues.sh ~945 LOC, 10 python3 heredocs + 11 check_* bash functions) to Python behind REBAR_COMPUTE, the second/last real port of Tier C (next-batch done in sure-beech-taunt). validate is repo-wide tracker health (takes NO ticket id), exit code score-encoded (exit == 5 - score). Unlike next-batch it is genuinely bash (per-check shell functions assembling finding buckets), so a heavier literal translation reusing rebar.reducer/rebar.graph.
## Scope

- New module(s) under rebar._engine_support (e.g. validate.py) reusing reduce_all_tickets/build_dep_graph; route ticket-reads.py validate + dispatcher arm via _compute_python (helper already added); library rebar.validate() + MCP in-process under the switch.

- Parity surface = finding bucketing + severity/ordering determinism (critical/major/minor/warning order, per-check emission order) and the score calculation + exit-code mapping. Pin with a byte-exact golden matrix (sorted ordering; no bash sort/set nondeterminism).
- Keep test files <1000 LOC; obey module-size policy (split heredoc clusters on real seams).
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] validate ported to rebar._engine_support, reusing reducer/graph (no re-implemented reducer)
- [ ] dispatcher + ticket-reads.py + library/MCP routed via REBAR_COMPUTE; default bash until flip+soak
- [ ] dual-run byte-identical (stdout/stderr + score-encoded exit) across bash|python for the golden matrix
- [ ] each check_* finding bucket + severity order pinned deterministically
- [ ] bash suite test-ticket-validate.sh passes under both switch values; new pytest gate added
- [ ] default stays bash pending flip+soak; validate-issues.sh retires with the tier

content.text: # Batch-commit write primitive for bulk operations (import/migration scale)
## Context

Discovered from the export/import design brainstorm (`rule-swamp-pupil`, 2026-06-17). rebar's write path commits ONE git commit per event under a per-event lock acquire/release (`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:82-146`, `src/rebar/_store/lock.py:187-231`); no batch/bulk/transaction primitive exists. A bulk import of N tickets composes N×~3 events → thousands of commits + thousands of lock cycles (~3.3 min of lock overhead alone for ~2,000 events, before commit/push). `rule-swamp-pupil` already mitigates network amplification (`REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` + single final push), but the per-event commit/lock cost remains. This task is the real fix, deferred until real-world store sizes demand it.
## What / Implementation detail

Add `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(events)`: under ONE write lock, write/rename M event files, one `git add`, ONE commit. Crucially, **each event remains its own file** (unique name) — so cross-clone union-merge and SNAPSHOT compaction (which key off event UUIDs, not commit boundaries) are unaffected, and invariants I5 (single locked writer) and I2 (composed events) hold. Route the importer (and optionally the reconciler applier) through it in chunks (per-ticket or per-N). Atomicity: a single commit of M staged files is atomic at the git level; partial staging before commit is recoverable because import is idempotent (resume by `source_id`).
## Success Criteria
- A bulk import of a large synthetic store completes in a fraction of the per-event wall-clock, with the same resulting logical state.
- Cross-clone convergence + compaction unaffected (regression tests).
- The single-event write path is unchanged.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(events)`: one lock, M event files, one commit; each event its own file
- [ ] importer routes events through it in chunks; single-event path unchanged
- [ ] atomicity + crash-resume (idempotent) tests; union-merge + compaction regression tests
- [ ] benchmark: large-import wall-clock before vs after
Deferred optimization for `rule-swamp-pupil`; build if store sizes demand it.
